604	\begin{lemma}
605	[Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes iff the localised complex is exact]
606	  \label{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}
607	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact,
608	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts,
609	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.isUnit_powers,
610	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.mul,
611	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.of_dvd,
612	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison,
613	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_apply,
614	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp,
615	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_apply,
616	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_comp_structure,
617	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_self,
618	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.comparison_unique,
619	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff,
620	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoeff_transport_eq_comparison,
621	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechCoface,
622	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechLocalized_exact,
623	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cechPrepend,
624	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hcomm,
625	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hsh,
626	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.cech_hu,
627	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod,
628	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_cons,
629	    AlgebraicGeometry.CechLocalized.sprod_succAbove_dvd}
630	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized, lem:section_cech_module_exact,
631	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
632	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
633	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
634	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is
635	  % the complex of localisations) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
636	  % L58--67).
637	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
638	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
639	  % is identified with the complex
640	  % $$
641	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
642	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
643	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
644	  % \ldots
645	  % $$"
646	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
647	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
648	  complex of localisations).}
649	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
650	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
651	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
652	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
653	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
654	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
655	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
656	  \[
657	    D^\bullet : \quad
658	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
659	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
660	    \cdots,
661	    \qquad
662	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
663	  \]
664	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
665	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
666	  \[
667	    1 \leq p
668	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
669	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
670	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
671	  \]
672	  which is the form the affine vanishing
673	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
674	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
675	  homomorphisms,
676	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
677	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
678	
679	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
680	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
681	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
682	  product}
683	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
684	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
685	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
686	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
687	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
688	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
689	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
690	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
691	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
692	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
693	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
694	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
695	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
696	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
697	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
698	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
699	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
700	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
701	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
702	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
703	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
704	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
705	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
706	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
707	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
708	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
709	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
710	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
711	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
712	\end{lemma}
713	
714	\begin{proof}
715	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
716	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
717	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
718	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
719	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
720	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
721	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
722	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
723	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
724	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
725	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
726	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
727	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
728	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
729	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
730	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
731	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
732	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
733	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
734	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
735	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
736	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
737	  element-level product equivalence
738	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
739	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
740	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
741	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
742	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
743	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
744	
745	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
746	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
747	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
748	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
749	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
750	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
751	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
752	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
753	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
754	  being proved.
755	\end{proof}

758	
759	\leanok
760	[Element-level product equivalence for the section {\v C}ech terms]
761	  \label{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}

763	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechProductEquiv_apply}

766	  abelian groups \(F_\sigma = \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) appearing in degree \(p\) of
767	  the section {\v C}ech complex of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}. There is
768	  an equivalence of underlying types
769	  \[
770	    \operatorname{ToType}\Bigl(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\Bigr)
771	    \;\simeq\;
772	    \prod_{\sigma} \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma),
773	  \]
774	  between the elements of the categorical product \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma} F_\sigma\) in
775	  \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the dependent functions \(\sigma \mapsto\) (element of
776	  \(F_\sigma\)). It is supplied by the Mathlib concrete-limit comparison
777	  \(\operatorname{CategoryTheory.Limits.Concrete.productEquiv}\), which holds because the
778	  forgetful functor \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete
779	  product (\(\operatorname{PreservesLimit}(\operatorname{Discrete.functor} F)\)). Under
780	  this equivalence each coordinate

782	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{dCoeff}\) of
783	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} by the
784	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) uniqueness of
785	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} (both
786	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and \(M_{s_\sigma}\) localise \(M\) at \(s_\sigma\), so
787	  the comparison is the canonical isomorphism). This is the move between the
788	  categorical product of \(\mathrm{Ab}\) and the per-\(\sigma\) localised modules.
789	  The coordinate projection of the equivalence is recorded separately as the
790	  definitional restatement
791	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\): the \(\sigma\)-component of the
792	  image of \(y\) under the equivalence is the underlying group map of the categorical
793	  projection \(\pi_\sigma\) applied to \(y\).

795	
796	\begin{proof}

798	  \leanok

800	  family \(\sigma \mapsto F_\sigma\); its hypothesis is that
801	  \(\operatorname{forget}\,\mathrm{Ab}\) preserves the discrete-diagram limit, which is
802	  the standard instance for \(\mathrm{Ab}\). The per-coordinate identification
803	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))) \cong M_{s_\sigma}\) is the
804	  uniqueness clause of \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\): by
805	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} the restriction map
806	  \(M \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localisation unit
807	  \(M \to M_{s_\sigma}\) are both localisations of \(M\) at
808	  \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\)
809	  (\(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\)), hence canonically isomorphic over
810	  \(M\). Assembling the coordinatewise isomorphisms after the product equivalence gives

814	\begin{lemma}
815	[Abstract cosimplicial unfold of the section {\v C}ech differential]
816	  \label{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}
817	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_objD_apply,
818	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechFaceRestr}
819	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
820	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a presheaf of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and
821	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an open cover. For a multi-index
822	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(q+2) \to I\) and a face index
823	  \(i \in \mathrm{Fin}(q+2)\), write
824	  \[
825	    \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i) :
826	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\Bigr)
827	      \;\longrightarrow\;
828	      \mathcal{F}\Bigl(\,\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)}\Bigr)
829	  \]
830	  for the \(i\)-th presheaf face restriction --- the value of \(\mathcal{F}\) on the
831	  inclusion of opens
832	  \(\bigcap_{k} U_{\sigma(k)} \subseteq \bigcap_{l} U_{(\sigma \circ d_i)(l)}\)
833	  obtained from the order-preserving coface \(d_i\) of the simplex category. Then,
834	  read through the product equivalence
835	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}\) of
836	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the underlying group action of the
837	  degree-\(q\) differential
838	  \(\operatorname{objD} q\) of the alternating-coface-map complex
839	  \(\operatorname{AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex}
840	  (\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\,\mathcal{U}\,\mathcal{F})\) is the alternating
841	  sum of the face restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates:
842	  \[
843	    \bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q+1}\,(\operatorname{objD} q\, t)\bigr)_\sigma
844	      \;=\;
845	      \sum_{i = 0}^{q+1} (-1)^i \,
846	        \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
847	          \Bigl(\bigl(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_{q}\, t\bigr)_{\sigma \circ d_i}\Bigr).
848	  \]
849	  This is \emph{purely cosimplicial}: it records the shape of the section {\v C}ech
850	  differential as an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions, with \emph{no}
851	  sheaf-theoretic or localisation identification of those restrictions. It is the
852	  abstract shape that the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
853	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} matches once the tilde-bridge
854	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match} is supplied.
855	\end{lemma}
856	
857	\begin{proof}
858	  \uses{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
859	  Unfold the degree-\(q\) map of \(\operatorname{alternatingCofaceMapComplex}\) on
860	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\): by construction \(\operatorname{objD} q\)
861	  is the alternating sum \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, d^i\) of the cosimplicial cofaces, each
862	  coface \(d^i\) being the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\) whose \(\sigma\)-component
863	  projects to the coordinate \(\sigma \circ d_i\) and post-composes with the presheaf
864	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\). Reading the
865	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate through the product equivalence (the coordinate-projection
866	  restatement \(\operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv\_apply}\)) commutes the projection
867	  past the categorical sum: applying the sum of \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-morphisms to an element
868	  distributes over the sum (the finite-sum-application identity
869	  \(\operatorname{ab\_hom\_finsetSum\_apply}\)), and each summand becomes
870	  \((-1)^i\) times the face restriction of the \((\sigma \circ d_i)\)-coordinate. The
871	  per-coordinate projection of the cosimplicial coface is the \(\operatorname{Pi.lift}\)
872	  computation \(\operatorname{Pi.lift\_π}\), which identifies the projected coface with
873	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) precomposed with the projection to
874	  \(\sigma \circ d_i\). Assembling the signs yields the stated alternating sum.
875	\end{proof}

877	\begin{lemma}
878	[Coface match: the section {\v C}ech differential is the localisation differential]
879	  \label{lem:section_cech_coface_match}
880	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch}
881	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_module_complex}
882	  Take \(X = \operatorname{Spec} R\), the standard affine cover \(\mathcal{U}\)
883	  associated with a spanning family \(s : \iota \to R\), and \(\mathcal{F} =
884	  \widetilde{M}\) the quasi-coherent sheaf associated with an \(R\)-module \(M\)
885	  (for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\), see the note following the statement). In
886	  the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}, the degree-\(p\)
887	  differential of the section {\v C}ech complex
888	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\), read through the product
889	  equivalence, is exactly the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of the
890	  module complex \(D^\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}.
891	  Concretely, under the per-coordinate identifications each section group
892	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\) and each face
893	  restriction \(\operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) is the away-localisation
894	  coface \(\operatorname{dCoface}\), so the alternating sum of face restrictions of
895	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} becomes the alternating sum of localisation
896	  cofaces, i.e.\ \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
897	\end{lemma}
898	
899	% NOTE: For the tilde case \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) the per-coordinate
900	% comparison isomorphism and the naturality square follow directly from
901	% \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\). For a general quasi-coherent
902	% \(\mathcal{F}\), reduce to the tilde case via \(\mathcal{F} \cong
903	% \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag 01I8), the affine equivalence
904	% \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\),
905	% which is the open gap recorded in Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
906	\begin{proof}
907	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, def:section_cech_module_complex, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
908	  The proof layers in two steps.
909	
910	  \emph{(i) Abstract unfold.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the
911	  \(\sigma\)-coordinate of the degree-\(p\) differential, read through the product
912	  equivalence, is the alternating sum
913	  \(\sum_{i} (-1)^i\, \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)\) of presheaf face
914	  restrictions applied to the deleted-face coordinates. This fixes the shape of the
915	  differential without any sheaf identification of the restrictions.
916	
917	  \emph{(ii) Tilde \(\mathcal{F}\)-bridge.} For \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) each
918	  face restriction is identified with the localisation coface
919	  \(\operatorname{SectionCechModule.dCoface}\) by transporting along a per-coordinate
920	  isomorphism. The section group
921	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) and the localised module
922	  \(M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}
923	  s_\sigma)\, M\) both localise \(M\) at the submonoid of powers of
924	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\): the former by
925	  \(\operatorname{qcohSectionsAwayLocalized}\) together with the multi-index
926	  intersection identity \(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\) (which rewrites
927	  \(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\) as the single basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\)), the latter
928	  by construction. Hence there is a canonical \(R\)-linear isomorphism
929	  \[
930	    \varphi_\sigma :
931	      \operatorname{ToType}\bigl(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\bigr)
932	      \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
933	      M_{s_\sigma},
934	  \]
935	  the comparison of the two \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) structures, unique by
936	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) and supplied by
937	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.iso}\). The naturality square
938	  \[
939	    \varphi_\sigma \;\circ\; \operatorname{sectionCechFaceRestr}(\sigma, i)
940	      \;=\;
941	      \operatorname{dCoface} \;\circ\; \varphi_{\sigma \circ d_i}
942	  \]
943	  is exactly \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\) of
944	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the presheaf restriction of
945	  \(\widetilde{M}\) between the two section groups is the away-localisation comparison
946	  of the two localisation units. Substituting (ii) into (i) and summing with signs
947	  identifies the section {\v C}ech differential with \(\operatorname{dDiff}\).
948	
949	  The isomorphism \(\varphi_\sigma\) is obtained by matching the two descriptions of
950	  the sections of \(\widetilde{M}\) over \(D(s_\sigma)\): the cosimplicial functor
951	  provides one description and the localisation property of
952	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} provides the other. Their agreement,
953	  together with the naturality square, is the content of this bridge.
954	\end{proof}

956	\begin{lemma}
957	[Abelian-group exactness criterion for the section {\v C}ech complex]
958	  \label{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}
959	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact,
960	    AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact,
961	    AlgebraicGeometry.ab_hom_finsetSum_apply}
962	  \uses{def:section_cech_complex, lem:section_cech_objd_apply,
963	    lem:section_cech_product_equiv, lem:section_cech_coface_match,
964	    lem:section_cech_module_exact}
965	  For \(1 \leq p\) the homology of the section {\v C}ech complex
966	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
967	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} vanishes,
968	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
969	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\). The argument splits into a homological
970	  precursor and an unconditional ladder transport.
971	  \begin{itemize}
972	    \item The precursor \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\)
973	      is pure homological algebra: \emph{given}
974	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of two
975	      consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
976	      \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\), the homology of the section complex at degree
977	      \(q+1\) is zero. It chains
978	      \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\),
979	      \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.exactAt\_iff'}\) (to expose the short complex
980	      around the node), and the abelian-group exactness criterion
981	      \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff\_function\_exact}\) (which states that
982	      a short complex of abelian groups is exact iff for every \(x_2\) with
983	      \(g(x_2) = 0\) there is \(x_1\) with \(f(x_1) = x_2\)).
984	    \item The unconditional \(\operatorname{sectionCechAbExact}\) discharges that
985	      hypothesis by transporting the already-proved \(R\)-module exactness
986	      \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
987	      Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the degreewise additive
988	      isomorphism, using the ladder-transport lemma
989	      \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\).
990	  \end{itemize}
991	\end{lemma}
992	
993	\begin{proof}
994	  \uses{lem:section_cech_objd_apply, lem:section_cech_product_equiv,
995	    lem:section_cech_coface_match, lem:section_cech_module_exact}
996	  Fix \(p = q + 1 \geq 1\). By the precursor
997	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_isZero\_homology\_of\_objD\_exact}\) it suffices to
998	  exhibit \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying group homomorphisms of the
999	  two consecutive coface differentials \(\operatorname{objD} q\),
1000	  \(\operatorname{objD}(q+1)\) of the section complex.
1001	
1002	  This exactness is obtained by the ladder transport
1003	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact.of\_ladder\_addEquiv\_of\_exact}\). The vertical maps
1004	  of the ladder are the degreewise additive isomorphisms
1005	  \(e_p = \prod_\sigma \varphi_\sigma \circ \operatorname{sectionCechProductEquiv}_p\):
1006	  the product equivalence of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv} followed by the
1007	  coordinatewise comparison \(\varphi_\sigma\) identifying each section group with the
1008	  localised module \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The two commuting squares of the ladder are the
1009	  coface match: the abstract unfold of the differential as an alternating sum of face
1010	  restrictions (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply}, the decl
1011	  \(\operatorname{sectionCech\_objD\_apply}\)) together with the per-coordinate
1012	  identification of each face restriction with the localisation coface
1013	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}) shows
1014	  \(e_{p+1} \circ \operatorname{objD} p = \operatorname{dDiff}^{\,p} \circ e_p\). The
1015	  horizontal exactness fed into the transport is the \(R\)-module exactness
1016	  \(H = \operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
1017	  Transporting \(H\) across the additive ladder yields the desired
1018	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the \(\operatorname{objD}\) maps, discharging the
1019	  hypothesis of the precursor.
1020	\end{proof}

1024	  \label{def:section_cech_module_complex}

1035	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff,
1036	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_apply,
1037	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_eq_depDiff,
1038	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.locDiff_exact,
1039	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.fLoc,

1045	    AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.spanIdx_spec,

1047	    AlgebraicGeometry.AwayComparison.Inverts.smul_pow_cancel}
1048	  \uses{def:standard_affine_cover, def:qcoh_sections_localized}
1049	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\)
1050	  the global sections of a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
1051	  R}\)-module \(\mathcal{F}\), and \(s : \iota \to R\) (\(\iota\) finite) a spanning
1052	  family of the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) =
1053	  \top\) (Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}). The \emph{un-localised section
1054	  {\v C}ech module complex} \(D^\bullet\) is the cochain complex of \(R\)-modules
1055	  whose degree-\(p\) term is the product over multi-indices
1056	  \(\sigma : \{0, \ldots, p\} \to \iota\) of the away localisations
1057	  \[
1058	    D^p \;=\; \prod_{\sigma} M_{s_\sigma},
1059	    \qquad s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
1060	    \qquad M_{s_\sigma} = \operatorname{LocalizedModule}
1061	      \bigl(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers}(s_\sigma)\bigr)\, M,
1062	  \]
1063	  with differential \(d^p : D^p \to D^{p+1}\) the alternating sum
1064	  \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j\, \delta^j\) of the coface comparison maps \(\delta^j\), each of
1065	  which is the canonical away-localisation comparison
1066	  \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\) sending a fraction to its image under
1067	  the further localisation at the omitted factor. The single-coefficient comparison
1068	  map and its localisation property are recorded by the \(\mathtt{dCoface}\) and
1069	  \(\mathtt{dCoeff}\) data and the differential by \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) (with action
1070	  \(\mathtt{dDiff\_apply}\)); the auxiliary \(\mathtt{spanIdx}\)/\(\mathtt{spanIdx\_spec}\)
1071	  select a witnessing index of the spanning relation.
1072	
1073	  The complex \(D^\bullet\) is tied to the section {\v C}ech complex of
1074	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} and to its localisations by three
1075	  comparison families. First, the maps \(\mathtt{dToCech}\) identify each term
1076	  \(D^p\) with the degree-\(p\) section group
1077	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) of
1078	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} as a localised module
1079	  (\(\mathtt{dToCech\_isLocalizedModule}\)), intertwining the differentials
1080	  (\(\mathtt{cechCoface\_dToCech}\), \(\mathtt{dToCech\_comm}\)); the section-side
1081	  cofaces are \(\mathtt{cechCofaceLin}\) with action \(\mathtt{cechCoface\_apply}\).
1082	  Second, localising \(D^\bullet\) at a spanning element \(s_r\) reproduces the
1083	  \emph{localised} {\v C}ech complex: the localised differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
1084	  (action \(\mathtt{locDiff\_apply}\)) agrees, via
1085	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\), with the dependent-coefficient combinatorial
1086	  differential, and its exactness is recorded as \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\). The
1087	  localisation comparison maps \(\mathtt{fLoc}\) (action \(\mathtt{fLoc\_apply}\))
1088	  exhibit each localised term as a localised module
1089	  (\(\mathtt{fLoc\_isLocalizedModule}\)) intertwining \(\mathtt{dDiff}\) with
1090	  \(\mathtt{locDiff}\) (\(\mathtt{locDiff\_fLoc}\), \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\)).
1091	  The key algebraic fact underlying all three families is the localisation
1092	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\): localising the away module \(M_{s_\sigma}\)
1093	  further at \(s_r\) gives \(M_{s_r s_\sigma}\). This is the keystone
1094	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\) (the composite of two away
1095	  localisations is again the away localisation at the product), with the cancellation
1096	  bookkeeping \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.Inverts.smul\_pow\_cancel}\). Thus localising
1097	  \(D^\bullet\) at \(s_r\) (an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule.Away}\)) reproduces
1098	  the localised Čech complex of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, and
1099	  exactness of \(D^\bullet\) can be checked one localisation at a time.
1100	\end{definition}

1103	
1104	\leanok
1105	[Positive-degree exactness of the section {\v C}ech module complex]
1106	  \label{lem:section_cech_module_exact}
1107	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact}
1108	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1109	  With the notation of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex}, the
1110	  un-localised section {\v C}ech module complex \(D^\bullet\) is exact in positive
1111	  degrees: for every \(p\),
1112	  \[
1113	    \operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(d^{p+1} : D^{p+1} \to D^{p+2},\;
1114	      d^{p+2} : D^{p+2} \to D^{p+3}\bigr).
1115	  \]
1116	  This is step~(a) of the affine vanishing: the node-by-node exactness of the
1117	  un-localised module complex, from which the homology vanishing of the section
1118	  {\v C}ech complex (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}, step~(c)) is read off.
1119	\end{lemma}
1120	
1121	\begin{proof}
1122	  \uses{def:section_cech_module_complex}
1123	  \leanok
1124	  Exactness of \(D^\bullet\) at each positive-degree node is a local-to-global
1125	  statement over the spanning family. By the local-to-global exactness criterion
1126	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\) (with the
1127	  spanning hypothesis \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\)), it
1128	  suffices to verify \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p+1}, d^{p+2})\) after
1129	  localising at each spanning element \(s_r\). In the localisation at \(s_r\) the
1130	  comparison family \(\mathtt{map\_dDiff\_eq\_locDiff}\) identifies the localised
1131	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) with the localised Čech differential \(\mathtt{locDiff}\)
1132	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_module_complex} (using the localisation
1133	  transitivity \(M_a[1/b] = M_{ab}\) of
1134	  \(\mathtt{AwayComparison.comparison\_isLocalizedModule}\)). Exactness of that
1135	  localised differential is \(\mathtt{locDiff\_exact}\), which is in turn the
1136	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial exactness transported along
1137	  \(\mathtt{locDiff\_eq\_depDiff}\); the contracting homotopy at the fixed index
1138	  \(r\) (where \(s_r\) is invertible) furnishes \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\),
1139	  giving the localised exactness one localisation at a time. Reassembling over the
1140	  spanning family yields the un-localised exactness.
1141	\end{proof}

1246	  global sections, and \((s : \iota \to R,\; hs : \operatorname{Ideal.span}
1247	  (\operatorname{range} s) = \top)\) for the spanning family of
1248	  Definition~\ref{def:standard_affine_cover}.
1249	
1250	  \paragraph{Reduction to the localised complex.}
1251	  By the section identification of Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, each
1252	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap D(s_{\sigma(p)})\)
1253	  is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
1254	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)}\), and its sections are the away localisation
1255	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma)) = M_{s_\sigma}\); under these identifications each face
1256	  restriction is the canonical localisation map. Hence
1257	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} identifies the section {\v C}ech complex
1258	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
1259	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} with the concrete complex of localised
1260	  \(R\)-modules
1261	  \[
1262	    \prod_{i_0} M_{s_{i_0}} \;\to\;
1263	    \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{s_{i_0} s_{i_1}} \;\to\;
1264	    \prod_{i_0 i_1 i_2} M_{s_{i_0} s_{i_1} s_{i_2}} \;\to\; \cdots,
1265	  \]
1266	  and reduces the vanishing
1267	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
1268	  \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p)\) for \(p > 0\) to positive-degree
1269	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) of the underlying localisation maps. Equivalently,
1270	  the extended complex \(0 \to M \to \prod_{i_0} M_{s_{i_0}} \to \cdots\) (whose
1271	  truncation is studied in Algebra, Lemma \texttt{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is
1272	  exact in positive degrees. The active target is the tilde case
1273	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), where the section identification of
1274	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} is gap-free and the coface match is
1275	  the abstract unfold of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} composed with the
1276	  tilde bridge of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}; a general quasi-coherent
1277	  \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case by \(\mathcal{F} \cong
1278	  \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the separately-deferred
1279	  globalisation gap.
1280	
1281	  \paragraph{Node-by-node exactness via local-to-global and the dependent port.}
1282	  Exactness of each positive-degree node is established from the spanning family
1283	  \((s, hs)\). The local-to-global exactness criterion
1284	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}(\operatorname{Set.range} s)\, hs\)
1285	  reduces \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(d^{p-1}, d^{p})\) to exactness after
1286	  localising at each spanning element \(r\) (the away localisation
1287	  \(\operatorname{Away}(s_r)\)), checking exactness one localisation at a time. In the
1288	  localisation at \(s_r\) the fixed index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} = r\) makes \(s_r\)
1289	  invertible, so the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) transport \(c\)
1290	  (\(\operatorname{depTransport}\)) is defined globally on that localisation and
1291	  furnishes a contracting homotopy of the localised complex: the unit, shift, and
1292	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\) (\(c \circ \delta_0 =
1293	  \operatorname{id}\)), \(\mathtt{hsh}\) (\(c \circ \delta_{k+1} = \delta_k \circ c\)),
1294	  and \(\mathtt{hcomm}\) hold for the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\). The node is
1295	  then closed by \(\operatorname{depDiff\_exact}\) of the dependent combinatorial port,
1296	  which packages the identity \((dh + hd) = \operatorname{id}\) into the
1297	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\) statement that
1298	  \(\operatorname{exact\_of\_isLocalized\_span}\) consumes. The constant- and
1299	  dependent-coefficient combinatorial cores and the spanning-family certifier are
1300	  already in place; the section and differential identifications of
1301	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and the homology--exactness reduction of
1302	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} are the bridge that connects them to the
1303	  abstract section complex.
1304	
1305	  The homotopy is built directly from the away-localisation identification, \emph{not}
1306	  routed through \(\operatorname{SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy}\): the
1307	  extra-degeneracy packaging of the contracting homotopy is a documented dead end for
1308	  this cover (the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map is only a section-level homotopy
1309	  after localisation, not an augmented-simplicial extra degeneracy), so the concrete
1310	  \(\operatorname{depHomotopy}\)/\(\operatorname{depDiff}\) route of the dependent port
1311	  is used instead.
1312	\end{proof}

251	/-- **Generator formula for `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The bijection
252	`Hom(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is evaluation of the morphism's component at `V` on the
253	canonical free generator `freeMk (𝟙 V)`. Recorded project-locally because this concrete
254	description is what makes the bijection visibly additive (`freeYonedaHomAddEquiv`) and is
255	unavailable as a packaged Mathlib lemma. -/
256	lemma freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (F : X.PresheafOfModules)
257	    (ψ : (PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) :
258	    freeYonedaHomEquiv V F ψ
259	      = (ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app (Opposite.op V))) (ModuleCat.freeMk (𝟙 V)) := by
260	  simp only [freeYonedaHomEquiv]; rfl
261	
262	/-- **Additive upgrade of `freeYonedaHomEquiv`.** The free–Yoneda hom bijection
263	`Hom_{PMod}(free(yoneda V), F) ≃ F(V)` is an isomorphism of abelian groups: the source is
264	the (preadditive) hom-group of presheaves of modules and the target is the section group
265	`F(V)`. This is the per-multi-index additive core of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
266	(blueprint `lem:cech_complex_hom_identification`); taking products over multi-indices and
267	intertwining with the differential upgrades it to the full complex isomorphism. Additivity
268	holds because, by `freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply`, the bijection is evaluation of the
269	component `ψ.app (op V)` (a module map, hence additive) on a fixed generator, and the
270	component of a sum of morphisms is the sum of components.
271	
272	Project-local because Mathlib packages the underlying bijection (`freeHomEquiv` ∘
273	`yonedaEquiv`) only at the level of types, not as an `AddEquiv`. -/
274	noncomputable def freeYonedaHomAddEquiv (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
275	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) :
276	    ((PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj).obj (yoneda.obj V) ⟶ F) ≃+
277	      (F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)) where
278	  toFun := freeYonedaHomEquiv V F
279	  invFun := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).symm
280	  left_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).left_inv
281	  right_inv := (freeYonedaHomEquiv V F).right_inv
282	  map_add' f g := by
283	    rw [freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply, freeYonedaHomEquiv_apply,
284	      PresheafOfModules.add_app]
285	    change (ModuleCat.Hom.hom (f.app (Opposite.op V) + g.app (Opposite.op V))) _ = _
286	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_add]; rfl
287	
288	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — section {\v C}ech complex -/
289	
290	/-- **Cosimplicial abelian group underlying the section {\v C}ech complex.**
291	
292	For a family of opens `U : ι → Opens X` (an open covering) and a presheaf of modules `F`,
293	the cosimplicial object in `Ab` whose value in cosimplicial degree `p` is the product
294	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` of sections over the `(p+1)`-fold intersections. A
295	cosimplicial map `f : [m] ⟶ [n]` acts at multi-index `σ : Fin (n+1) → ι` by projecting to
296	the coordinate `σ ∘ f` and then restricting along `⨅ₖ U (σ k) ≤ ⨅ₗ U (σ (f l))`. This is the
297	covariant ({\v C}ech) variance that the alternating-coface-map complex turns into a cochain
298	complex.
299	
300	Project-local: Mathlib has no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules. Built
301	as an honest `SimplexCategory ⥤ Ab` functor so that `alternatingCofaceMapComplex` supplies
302	`d² = 0` for free (no hand-rolled alternating-sum identity). -/
303	noncomputable def sectionCechCosimplicial {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
304	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CosimplicialObject Ab.{u} where
305	  obj n := ∏ᶜ (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι => F.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, U (σ k))))
306	  map {m n} f := Pi.lift (fun σ : Fin (n.len + 1) → ι =>
307	    Pi.π _ (σ ∘ f.toOrderHom) ≫
308	      F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ (f.toOrderHom l)))).op)
309	  map_id n := by
310	    apply Pi.hom_ext
311	    intro σ
312	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, SimplexCategory.id_toOrderHom,
313	      OrderHom.id_coe, Function.comp_id]
314	    rw [Subsingleton.elim (homOfLE _).op (𝟙 _)]
315	    simp
316	  map_comp {l m n} f g := by
317	    apply Pi.hom_ext
318	    intro σ
319	    simp only [Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, SimplexCategory.comp_toOrderHom, OrderHom.comp_coe]
320	    rw [← Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp]
321	    congr 1
322	
323	/-- **Section {\v C}ech cochain complex of a presheaf of modules**
324	(blueprint `def:section_cech_complex`).
325	
326	The cochain complex `Č•(𝒰, F)` of abelian groups with degree-`p` term
327	`∏_{σ : Fin (p+1) → ι} F(⨅ₖ U (σ k))` and the alternating-sum-of-restrictions differential,
328	obtained as the alternating-coface-map complex of `sectionCechCosimplicial`. This is the
329	section complex of the blueprint, kept distinct from the relative pushforward `CechComplex`;
330	it is the right-hand side of the {\v C}ech hom-identification
331	`Hom_{PMod}(K(𝒰)_•, F) ≅ Č•(𝒰, F)`.
332	
333	Project-local: no {\v C}ech complex of sections of a presheaf of modules exists in Mathlib. -/
334	noncomputable def sectionCechComplex {ι : Type u} (U : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
335	    (F : X.PresheafOfModules) : CochainComplex Ab.{u} ℕ :=
336	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex Ab.{u}).obj (sectionCechCosimplicial U F)
337	
338	end AlgebraicGeometry

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.PresheafCech
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry
open Scheme.Modules

-- the tilde presheaf of modules
noncomputable example (R : CommRingCat) (M : ModuleCat R) :
    (Spec R).PresheafOfModules :=
  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)

-- per-σ AddEquiv (the φ_σ)
noncomputable example (R : CommRingCat) (M : ModuleCat R) {ι : Type _} (s : ι → R)
    {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ LocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k))) M := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  exact (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).toAddEquiv.symm

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — tilde-bridge and affine vanishing (L1 steps A, B, d)

The final stretch of the L1 categorical→module bridge for the **tilde sheaf**
`F = ~M`.  The previous section moved the abstract `Ab`-valued section {\v C}ech complex
to the shape of an alternating sum of presheaf restrictions
(`sectionCech_objD_apply`); here we identify, degreewise, those section groups with the
away-localisation coefficients `dCoeff` and the restrictions with the localisation
cofaces `dCoface`, via the per-σ comparison `φ_σ` (`IsLocalizedModule.iso`).  Transporting
the already-proven module exactness `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the resulting
degreewise additive isomorphism (`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`) discharges
the underlying-group exactness, which `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact` turns
into positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech complex
(`sectionCech_affine_vanishing`). -/

section SectionCechTilde

open AlgebraicTopology Scheme.Modules SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)

/-- The presheaf of `O_{Spec R}`-modules underlying the tilde sheaf `~M`; the section
{\v C}ech complex of the affine vanishing is taken on this presheaf. -/
private noncomputable abbrev tP : (Spec R).PresheafOfModules :=
  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)

/-- The standard basic-open cover family `i ↦ D(s i)`. -/
private abbrev tU : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R) :=
  fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)

/-- The per-σ comparison `R`-linear isomorphism between the (accessor-2 `ModuleCat`)
sections of `~M` over the {\v C}ech intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` and the away
localisation `M_{s_σ} = dCoeff`.  Both localise `M` at `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`
(`qcohSectionsAwayLocalized`), so they are canonically isomorphic via
`IsLocalizedModule.iso`. -/
private noncomputable def phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm

/-- The additive per-σ comparison `φ_σ`, the underlying `AddEquiv` of `phiL`, stated on
the accessor-1 (`Ab`-valued) section group so it composes with `sectionCechProductEquiv`. -/
private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv

end SectionCechTilde

end AlgebraicGeometry

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules AlgebraicTopology
open SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison

variable {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)

-- iso_symm_comp pointwise
example {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) (m : M) :
    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm
        ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m)
      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k))) M m := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l : _ → _))
    (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom)) m

-- tilde.toOpen_res pointwise: faceRestr (toOpen source m) = toOpen target m
example {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) (m : M) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
        ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))).hom m)
      = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m := by
  have h := tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
      (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
      (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
      (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l))))
  exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _)) h) m

private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv

/-- `φ_σ` carries the localisation unit `toOpen M (D s_σ)` of `M` to the canonical
`LocalizedModule` unit — the defining `iso_symm_comp` property of the comparison. -/
private lemma phiL_toOpen {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) (m : M) :
    phiL M s σ ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m)
      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k))) M m := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l : _ → _))
    (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom)) m

/-- **Abstract restriction bridge.** For any inclusion of opens, the accessor-1
(`Ab`-valued) presheaf restriction of `tP = ~M` and the accessor-2 (`ModuleCat`-valued)
restriction of `modulesSpecToSheaf (~M)` agree on underlying functions.  Stated for an
abstract morphism `g` so that the (otherwise heartbeat-exploding) defeq is checked once,
abstractly, rather than on the concrete {\v C}ech inclusion. -/
private lemma restr_bridge (V W : (Spec R).Opens) (g : V ⟶ W)
    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom ((tP M).presheaf.map g.op) x
      = (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map g.op).hom) x := rfl

/-- **Linear-level per-coface naturality** (`R`-linear).  The comparison `φ_σ`
intertwines the (accessor-2) {\v C}ech face restriction with the away-localisation coface
`dCoface`.  Proved by `IsLocalizedModule.ext`: both composites send the localisation unit
`toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})` of `M` to the localisation unit `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`, by
`tilde.toOpen_res` (restriction commutes with `toOpen`), `phiL_toOpen`
(`iso_symm_comp`), and `comparison_apply` (the defining property of `dCoface`). -/
private lemma phiL_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
    (phiL M s σ).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ (phiL M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)).toLinearMap := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
  refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom
    (fun x => (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ i)
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)).isUnit_powers x) ?_
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro m
  simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
  -- the {\v C}ech face restriction sends `toOpen (source)` to `toOpen (target)`
  have hres : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
        ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
      = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m :=
    congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))
      (tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m
  rw [hres, phiL_toOpen, phiL_toOpen, dCoface]
  exact (comparison_apply _ _ _ m).symm

/-- **Additive per-coface naturality** (accessor-1, the form the coface match consumes).
The additive comparison `φ_σ` intertwines the accessor-1 {\v C}ech face restriction
`sectionCechFaceRestr` with the away-localisation coface `dCoface`.  Obtained from the
linear naturality `phiL_naturality` via the accessor bridge `restr_bridge`. -/
private lemma phi_naturality {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2))
    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op
        (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))))) :
    phi M s σ (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr (tU s) (tP M) σ i) x)
      = dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i (phi M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove) x) := by
  show phiL M s σ (ConcreteCategory.hom (sectionCechFaceRestr (tU s) (tP M) σ i) x)
      = dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i (phiL M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove) x)
  rw [sectionCechFaceRestr, restr_bridge]
  exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l : _ → _)) (phiL_naturality M s σ i)) x

end SectionCechTilde

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.CechAcyclic
open CategoryTheory Limits AlgebraicGeometry Scheme.Modules AlgebraicTopology
open SectionCechModule CechLocalized AwayComparison

set_option maxHeartbeats 1000000 in
example {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) {ι : Type u} (s : ι → R)
    {q : ℕ} (σ : Fin (q + 2) → ι) (i : Fin (q + 2)) :
    haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
    (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
        (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap ∘ₗ
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ
        (haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
         (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
          (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom).symm.toLinearMap) := by
  haveI i1 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  haveI i2 := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
  refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom
    (fun x => (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ i)
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)).isUnit_powers x) ?_
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro m
  simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
  have hres : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
        ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
      = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m :=
    congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))
      (tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m
  rw [hres]
  rw [DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom) m]
  rw [DFunLike.congr_fun (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp
      (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom) m]
  exact (comparison_apply
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ i.succAbove))) M)
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M) _ m).symm

    (phiL M s σ).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
          (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _
            ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
      = (dCoface s M (q + 1) σ i) ∘ₗ (phiL M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)).toLinearMap := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)
  refine IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom
    (fun x => (Inverts.of_dvd (sprod_succAbove_dvd s σ i)
      (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M)).isUnit_powers x) ?_
  apply LinearMap.ext
  intro m
  simp only [LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe]
  -- the {\v C}ech face restriction sends `toOpen (source)` to `toOpen (target)`
  have hres : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom
        ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom m)
      = (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m :=
    congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))
      (tilde.toOpen_res M (⨅ l, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen
          (s ((σ ∘ (SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) l)))
        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m
  rw [hres, phiL_toOpen M s σ, phiL_toOpen M s (σ ∘ i.succAbove)]
  exact (comparison_apply
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s (σ ∘ i.succAbove))) M)
    (LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (sprod s σ)) M) _ m).symm

/-- The presheaf of `O_{Spec R}`-modules underlying the tilde sheaf `~M`; the section
{\v C}ech complex of the affine vanishing is taken on this presheaf. -/
private noncomputable abbrev tP : (Spec R).PresheafOfModules :=
  (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M)

/-- The standard basic-open cover family `i ↦ D(s i)`. -/
private abbrev tU : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R) :=
  fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i)

/-- The per-σ comparison `R`-linear isomorphism between the (accessor-2 `ModuleCat`)
sections of `~M` over the {\v C}ech intersection `⨅ₖ D(s_{σ k})` and the away
localisation `M_{s_σ} = dCoeff`.  Both localise `M` at `Submonoid.powers (∏ₖ s_{σ k})`
(`qcohSectionsAwayLocalized`), so they are canonically isomorphic via
`IsLocalizedModule.iso`. -/
private noncomputable def phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.obj
        (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃ₗ[R] dCoeff s M σ :=
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm

/-- The additive per-σ comparison `φ_σ`, the underlying `AddEquiv` of `phiL`, stated on
the accessor-1 (`Ab`-valued) section group so it composes with `sectionCechProductEquiv`. -/
private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv

/-- `φ_σ` carries the localisation unit `toOpen M (D s_σ)` of `M` to the canonical
`LocalizedModule` unit — the defining `iso_symm_comp` property of the comparison. -/
private lemma phiL_toOpen {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) (m : M) :
    phiL M s σ ((tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom m)
      = LocalizedModule.mkLinearMap (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k))) M m := by
  haveI := qcohSectionsAwayLocalized M s σ
  exact DFunLike.congr_fun
    (IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_comp (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
      (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom) m

/-- **Abstract restriction bridge.** For any inclusion of opens, the accessor-1
(`Ab`-valued) presheaf restriction of `tP = ~M` and the accessor-2 (`ModuleCat`-valued)
restriction of `modulesSpecToSheaf (~M)` agree on underlying functions.  Stated for an
abstract morphism `g` so that the (otherwise heartbeat-exploding) defeq is checked once,
abstractly, rather than on the concrete {\v C}ech inclusion. -/
private lemma restr_bridge (V W : (Spec R).Opens) (g : V ⟶ W)
    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom ((tP M).presheaf.map g.op) x
      = (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map g.op).hom) x := rfl

  (IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers (∏ k, s (σ k)))
    (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))).hom).symm

/-- The additive per-σ comparison `φ_σ`, the underlying `AddEquiv` of `phiL`, stated on
the accessor-1 (`Ab`-valued) section group so it composes with `sectionCechProductEquiv`. -/
private noncomputable def phi {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι) :
    ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))
      ≃+ dCoeff s M σ :=
  (phiL M s σ).toAddEquiv

/-- The raw `IsLocalizedModule.iso ▸ symm` underlying `phi` agrees with `phiL` applied —
the definitional bridge used to feed `phiL_naturality` into `phi_naturality`. -/
private lemma phi_eq_phiL {n : ℕ} (σ : Fin n → ι)
    (y : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))) :
    phi M s σ y = phiL M s σ y := rfl

/-- **Abstract restriction bridge.** For any inclusion of opens, the accessor-1
(`Ab`-valued) presheaf restriction of `tP = ~M` and the accessor-2 (`ModuleCat`-valued)
restriction of `modulesSpecToSheaf (~M)` agree on underlying functions.  Stated for an
abstract morphism `g` so that the (otherwise heartbeat-exploding) defeq is checked once,
abstractly, rather than on the concrete {\v C}ech inclusion. -/
private lemma restr_bridge (V W : (Spec R).Opens) (g : V ⟶ W)
    (x : ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))) :
    ConcreteCategory.hom ((tP M).presheaf.map g.op) x
      = (((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).presheaf.map g.op).hom) x := rfl

        ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))).op).hom with hg_def
  -- the face restriction commutes with `toOpen` (as `R`-linear maps), via `tilde.toOpen_res`
  have hg : g ∘ₗ (tilde.toOpen M (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s ((σ ∘ i.succAbove) k)))).hom

    exact congrFun (congrArg (fun l => (l.hom : _ → _))

        (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))
        (homOfLE (le_iInf (fun l => iInf_le _ ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom l)))))) m

/-- **Additive product equivalence** for the degree-`p` section {\v C}ech term: the
underlying `AddEquiv` upgrade of `sectionCechProductEquiv` (additive because each
coordinate is the underlying group map of a categorical projection `Pi.π`). -/
private noncomputable def sectionProdAddEquiv (p : ℕ) :
    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p))
      ≃+ (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι,
            ToType ((tP M).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨅ k, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s (σ k)))))) where
  toFun := sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p
  invFun := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).symm
  left_inv := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).left_inv
  right_inv := (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p).right_inv
  map_add' x y := by
    funext σ
    simp only [sectionCechProductEquiv_apply, Pi.add_apply, map_add]

/-- The degree-`p` comparison `AddEquiv` `ToType(∏ᶜ_σ ~M(D s_σ)) ≃+ ∏_σ M_{s_σ}`: the
additive product equivalence followed by the coordinatewise comparison `φ_σ`.  These are
the vertical maps of the ladder transporting `dDiff`-exactness to `objD`-exactness. -/
private noncomputable def sectionToModuleAddEquiv (p : ℕ) :
    ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p))
      ≃+ (∀ σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ) :=
  (sectionProdAddEquiv M s p).trans (AddEquiv.piCongrRight (fun σ => phi M s σ))

/-- Coordinate description of `sectionToModuleAddEquiv`. -/
private lemma sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply (p : ℕ)
    (y : ToType ((sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)).obj (SimplexCategory.mk p)))
    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → ι) :
    sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s p y σ = phi M s σ (sectionCechProductEquiv (tU s) (tP M) p y σ) :=

end SectionCechTilde

/-- Reading the inverse comparison coordinatewise: applying `sectionToModuleAddEquiv.symm`

private lemma sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply (q : ℕ) (z : ∀ σ : Fin (q + 1) → ι, dCoeff s M σ)

refine Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => ?_)

/-- **(c, ladder transport) Underlying-group exactness** of two consecutive section {\v C}ech
coface differentials (`lem:section_cech_ab_exact`).  Transports the `R`-module exactness
`SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across the degreewise additive comparison
`sectionToModuleAddEquiv` (the squares are `sectionCechCofaceMatch`) via
`Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`. -/
private lemma sectionCechAbExact [Finite ι] (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤) (q : ℕ) :
    Function.Exact
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) q))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
        (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) (q + 1))) := by
  have sq : ∀ r : ℕ,
      (ConcreteCategory.hom
            (AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD (sectionCechCosimplicial (tU s) (tP M)) r)).comp
          (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s r).symm.toAddMonoidHom
        = (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (r + 1)).symm.toAddMonoidHom.comp
            (dDiff s M (r + 1)).toAddMonoidHom := by
    intro r
    apply AddMonoidHom.ext
    intro z
    simp only [AddMonoidHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, AddEquiv.coe_toAddMonoidHom,
      LinearMap.toAddMonoidHom_coe]
    rw [AddEquiv.eq_symm_apply]
    exact sectionCechCofaceMatch M s r z
  exact Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact
    (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s q).symm (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 1)).symm
    (sectionToModuleAddEquiv M s (q + 2)).symm (sq q) (sq (q + 1)) (dDiff_exact s M hs q)

end SectionCechTilde

/-- **Section {\v C}ech homology vanishes in positive degrees** (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`),
tilde case `F = ~M`.  Reads off positive-degree homology vanishing of the section {\v C}ech
complex from the underlying-group exactness `sectionCechAbExact` via the homological precursor
`sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`. -/
theorem sectionCech_homology_exact {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p) := by
  obtain ⟨q, rfl⟩ : ∃ q, p = q + 1 := ⟨p - 1, by omega⟩
  exact sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact _ _ q (sectionCechAbExact M s hs q)

/-- **Standard-cover {\v C}ech vanishing on affines, section form** (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine`,
section form).  For a spanning family `s : ι → R` and the tilde sheaf `~M` of an `R`-module
`M`, the section {\v C}ech complex of the associated standard affine cover has vanishing
cohomology in all positive degrees.  This is the named affine-vanishing target; the general
quasi-coherent `F` is reduced to this tilde case via `F ≅ ~(ΓF)` (Stacks 01I8, deferred). -/
theorem sectionCech_affine_vanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R)
    {ι : Type u} [Finite ι] (s : ι → R) (hs : Ideal.span (Set.range s) = ⊤)
    (p : ℕ) (hp : 1 ≤ p) :
    IsZero ((sectionCechComplex (fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (s i))
      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (tilde M))).homology p) :=
  sectionCech_homology_exact M s hs p hp

# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added: 16** (all axiom-clean: `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`),
  in a new `section SectionCechTilde` + two public theorems after it.
- **Declarations blocked: 0.** Lane 2 (P3 L1 tilde-bridge + ladder + step-d) is **COMPLETE**.
- **Project sorry count: 1 → 1** (unchanged). The only `sorry` is the pre-existing,
  superseded relative-form `CechAcyclic.affine` (line 110), which PROGRESS instructs to
  leave as-is. **No new sorry.**
- The named affine-vanishing target **`AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_affine_vanishing`**
  (blueprint `lem:cech_acyclic_affine`, section form) and
  **`AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact`** (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`)
  are both **proven, axiom-clean, and resolve cross-module**.

## What was built (the full L1 tilde-bridge, steps A/B/d)

Following `analogies/tilde-section-bridge.md` (the blocker dissolves to `rfl`/defeq, keep
the `Ab`-valued section complex) and the PROGRESS Lane-2 recipe. Bottom-up:

1. `phiL_naturality` (private lemma) — **the bottleneck**, linear-level per-coface
   naturality `φ_σ ∘ faceRestr = dCoface ∘ φ_{σ∘dᵢ}` via `IsLocalizedModule.ext`,
   `tilde.toOpen_res`, `iso_symm_comp`, `comparison_apply`.
2. `tP`, `tU` (private abbrev) — the tilde presheaf `(toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj (~M)`
   and the basic-open cover `i ↦ D(s i)`.
3. `phiL` (private def) — per-σ `R`-linear comparison `~M(⨅ₖ D s_{σk}) ≃ₗ M_{s_σ}` via
   `IsLocalizedModule.iso`; `phi` (private def) — its additive form on the accessor-1
   (`Ab`) section group; `phi_eq_phiL` (defeq bridge).
4. `restr_bridge` (private lemma) — accessor-1 (`Ab`) vs accessor-2 (`ModuleCat`)
   restriction agree (rfl on an **abstract** inclusion to dodge the heartbeat blowup).
5. `phi_naturality` (private lemma) — additive accessor-1 naturality, from (1)+(4).
6. `sectionProdAddEquiv`, `sectionToModuleAddEquiv` (private def) +
   `sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply`, `sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply` — the degreewise
   additive comparison `ToType(∏ᶜ_σ ~M(D s_σ)) ≃+ ∏_σ M_{s_σ}` (ladder vertical maps).
7. `sectionCechCofaceMatch` (private lemma, **`lem:section_cech_coface_match`**) — the
   ladder square `objD` ↔ `dDiff`, from `sectionCech_objD_apply` + `phi_naturality`.
8. `sectionCechAbExact` (private lemma, **`lem:section_cech_ab_exact`**) — underlying-group
   exactness of consecutive `objD`, by `Function.Exact.of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact`
   transporting `SectionCechModule.dDiff_exact` across `sectionToModuleAddEquiv`.
9. `sectionCech_homology_exact` (public theorem, **`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`**) —
   `IsZero (homology p)` for `p ≥ 1`, via `sectionCech_isZero_homology_of_objD_exact`.
10. `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` (public theorem, **`lem:cech_acyclic_affine` §section**)
    — the named target, `= sectionCech_homology_exact` (tilde case).

## Per-declaration notes

### phiL_naturality (RESOLVED, axiom-clean)
- **Approach:** `IsLocalizedModule.ext` on the localisation unit `toOpen M (D s_{σ∘dᵢ})`;
  both composites land on `mkLinearMap (powers s_σ)`.
- **Critical performance lesson:** the accessor-2 `modulesSpecToSheaf (~M)` section types
  are **defeq-intensive** — a naive `IsLocalizedModule.ext` over the concrete face
  restriction times out even at 2 000 000 heartbeats. **Fix:** `set g := (… restriction …).hom`
  then `clear_value g` BEFORE `ext`, so `ext` never whnf's the concrete map. With the map
  opaque the proof closes at 1 000 000 heartbeats.
- **Second lesson:** the goal carries `↑(iso).symm ∘ₗ g` (a `LinearEquiv` coerced to
  `LinearMap`). `rw`/`simp [LinearMap.comp_apply / coe_comp]` and `LinearMap.comp_assoc`
  **silently fail to match** this (semilinear `RingHomCompTriple` instance mismatch). Use
  `change` to the defeq fully-applied form, then `rw` the applied (`DFunLike.congr_fun`)
  versions of `iso_symm_comp`. Do NOT try `rw [comp_assoc]` / `rw [comp_apply]` on a
  LinearEquiv-coerced composition — it is a dead end (documented, attempted, abandoned).
- **In-file vs scratch:** identical proof is ~5× slower in-file than via an imported
  `.olean` scratch; budget accordingly.

### restr_bridge (RESOLVED)
- **Approach:** `rfl` on an **abstract** open inclusion `g`. The same `rfl` on the concrete
  `homOfLE (le_iInf …)` inclusion blows up `whnf`. Always invoke the (1)↔(2) accessor
  bridge with abstract arguments.

### sectionCechCofaceMatch / sectionCechAbExact / homology_exact / affine_vanishing
- **Approach:** as in the recipe; all RESOLVED, axiom-clean. The two squares for
  `of_ladder_addEquiv_of_exact` come from `sectionCechCofaceMatch` via
  `AddEquiv.eq_symm_apply`.

## Needs blueprint entry (MANDATORY — restore 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint)

The following **new non-pinned helper declarations** were added and are NOT yet in any
blueprint `\lean{…}` list (they are `lean_aux` nodes; `archon dag-query unmatched` will
list them). All are `private` but resolve by qualified name `AlgebraicGeometry.<name>`
(verified — exactly like the existing private `CombinatorialCech.combDifferential`). Bundle
each into the `\lean{…}` list of the indicated blueprint declaration:

- Into `def:qcoh_sections_localized` (tilde-bridge accessors) or a new `def:section_tilde_bridge`:
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.tP`, `AlgebraicGeometry.tU` (private abbrev — presheaf + cover)
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.phiL`, `AlgebraicGeometry.phi`, `AlgebraicGeometry.phi_eq_phiL`
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.restr_bridge`
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.phiL_naturality`, `AlgebraicGeometry.phi_naturality`
- Into `lem:section_cech_product_equiv` (additive product machinery):
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdAddEquiv`
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv`, `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply`
  - `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply`

Already-pinned (no action needed, ready for `\leanok` by sync_leanok):
- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechCofaceMatch` (`lem:section_cech_coface_match`)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCechAbExact` (`lem:section_cech_ab_exact`)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_homology_exact` (`lem:section_cech_homology_exact`)
- `AlgebraicGeometry.sectionCech_affine_vanishing` (`lem:cech_acyclic_affine` §section form)

## Module-doc accuracy note (for review)
The deferred note in PROGRESS ("CechAcyclic module-doc overstatement … the full coface
assembly is present when only the abstract half is") is now **accurate**: this iter closes
the full coface assembly (A/B) and the homology-vanishing reduction.

## Why I stopped
**Real progress: 16 axiom-clean declarations added; Lane 2 fully closed.**
- `phiL_naturality` (line ~1352), `tP`/`tU` (~1409/1414), `phiL`/`phi` (~1425/1438),
  `phi_eq_phiL` (~1456), `restr_bridge` (~1465), `phi_naturality` (~1475),
  `sectionProdAddEquiv` (~1490), `sectionToModuleAddEquiv` (~1505),
  `sectionToModuleAddEquiv_apply` (~1518), `sectionProdEquiv_symm_apply` (~1524),
  `sectionCechCofaceMatch` (~1538), `sectionCechAbExact` (~1556),
