1	\chapter{{\v C}ech computation of higher direct images $R^i f_*$ (unconditional)}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechAcyclic.lean
5	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/PresheafCech.lean
6	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/FreePresheafComplex.lean
7	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechBridge.lean
8	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean
9	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
10	% quasi-coherent sheaves and \S Quasi-coherence of higher direct images
11	% (Lemmas \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial},
12	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero},
13	% \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent},
14	% \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}) (read from
15	% references/stacks-coherent.tex).
16	
17	\section{Setup and motivation}
18	
19	This chapter constructs the higher derived direct images \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) for
20	\(i \geq 1\) \emph{without appealing to injective resolutions in the category of
21	sheaves of modules}. The companion development of
22	Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_HigherDirectImage} defines \(R^i f_*\) as a right
23	derived functor via injective resolutions, which requires the ambient category of
24	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules to have enough injectives. The {\v C}ech approach
25	developed here sidesteps this requirement:
26	it computes \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) as the cohomology of an explicit complex built
27	from the pushforwards of \(\mathcal{F}\) over the finite intersections of an affine
28	open cover, and so produces an \emph{unconditional} construction of \(R^i f_*\) for
29	quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) and separated quasi-compact \(f\).
30	
31	Throughout, \(f : X \to S\) is a morphism of schemes that is quasi-compact and
32	separated (so that all finite intersections of an affine open cover of \(X\) are
33	again affine), and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. We
34	write \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) for the category of quasi-coherent
35	\(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. A base change of \(f\) along a morphism \(g : S' \to S\)
36	is recorded by the cartesian square obtained from the fibre product
37	\(X' = X \times_S S'\):
38	\[
39	  \begin{array}{ccc}
40	    X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
41	    {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
42	    S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
43	  \end{array}
44	\]
45	Here \(f' : X' \to S'\) is the base change of \(f\), \(g' : X' \to X\) is the first
46	projection, and \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the pullback of
47	\(\mathcal{F}\) to \(X'\).
48	
49	\section{The {\v C}ech nerve and {\v C}ech complex of an affine cover}
50	
51	\begin{definition}
52	
53	\leanok
54	[{\v C}ech nerve of an affine open cover]
55	  \label{def:cech_nerve}
56	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechNerve}
57	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor, def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
58	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
59	  % quasi-coherent sheaves, opening paragraph and Lemma
60	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial}
61	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L36--67).
62	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
63	  % Let $U \subset X$ be an affine open.
64	  % Recall that a {\it standard open covering} of $U$ is a covering
65	  % of the form $\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i = 1}^n D(f_i)$
66	  % where $f_1, \ldots, f_n \in \Gamma(U, \mathcal{O}_X)$ generate
67	  % the unit ideal, see
68	  % Schemes, Definition \ref{schemes-definition-standard-covering}."
69	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, \S{\v C}ech cohomology of
70	  quasi-coherent sheaves.}
71	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let
72	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite open cover of \(X\) by
73	  affine opens, with index set \(I\). For a finite nonempty tuple
74	  \(i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I\) write
75	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = U_{i_0} \cap \cdots \cap U_{i_p}\) for the corresponding
76	  intersection. The \emph{{\v C}ech nerve} of \(\mathcal{U}\) is the augmented
77	  \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\) whose object in
78	  cosimplicial degree \(p\) is the direct image, along the open immersions
79	  \(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p} : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\), of the restriction
80	  of \(\mathcal{O}_X\) (or of any chosen quasi-coherent sheaf) to the
81	  \((p+1)\)-fold intersections,
82	  \[
83	    [p] \;\longmapsto\;
84	      \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
85	        (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_* \,\bigl(\,\cdot\,|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr),
86	  \]
87	  with cofaces given by the restriction maps that omit one index and
88	  codegeneracies by the maps that repeat one index; the augmentation in degree
89	  \(-1\) is the object on all of \(X\). The variance is essential: the geometric
90	  nerve of the cover (the iterated fibre powers of \(\coprod_i U_i\) over \(X\),
91	  introduced in \S\ref{sec:cech_three_part} below) is a \emph{simplicial} object
92	  in \emph{schemes}, but applying the covariant direct-image functor turns it into
93	  a \emph{cosimplicial} object in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); consequently the
94	  associated alternating-coface complex is a \emph{cochain} complex (differentials
95	  raising degree), not a chain complex. When \(X\) is separated each intersection
96	  \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is itself affine. The local affine model is the
97	  \emph{standard open covering} \(U = \bigcup_{i=1}^n D(f_i)\) of an affine open
98	  \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) by basic opens, where \(f_1, \ldots, f_n \in A\)
99	  generate the unit ideal; on such a cover the intersections are again basic opens
100	  \(D(f_{i_0} \cdots f_{i_p})\).
101	\end{definition}
102	
103	\begin{definition}
104	
105	\leanok
106	[{\v C}ech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf]
107	  \label{def:cech_complex}
108	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.CechComplex}
109	  \uses{def:cech_nerve, def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
110	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
111	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (proof)
112	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L59--77).
113	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
114	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
115	  % is identified with the complex
116	  % $$
117	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
118	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
119	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
120	  % \ldots
121	  % $$
122	  % We are asked to show that the extended complex
123	  % \begin{equation}
124	  % \label{equation-extended}
125	  % 0 \to
126	  % M \to
127	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
128	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
129	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to
130	  % \ldots
131	  % \end{equation}
132	  % (whose truncation we have studied in
133	  % Algebra, Lemma \ref{algebra-lemma-cover-module}) is exact."
134	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
135	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (standard-cover {\v C}ech
136	  vanishing).}
137	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and let
138	  \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover with
139	  all intersections \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) affine (e.g.\ \(X\) separated). The
140	  \emph{{\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
141	  is the cochain complex obtained by applying the global-sections functor to the
142	  {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\);
143	  in degree \(p\) it is
144	  \[
145	    \check{\mathcal{C}}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})
146	      = \prod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
147	        \mathcal{F}\bigl(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\bigr),
148	  \]
149	  with differential \(d : \check{\mathcal{C}}^p \to \check{\mathcal{C}}^{p+1}\) the
150	  alternating sum \((d s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\,
151	  s_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}}\) of the
152	  restriction maps. Over an affine \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) with
153	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U = \widetilde{M}\) and a standard cover by the
154	  \(D(f_i)\), this is the complex
155	  \(\prod_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to \prod_{i_0 i_1} M_{f_{i_0} f_{i_1}} \to \cdots\) of
156	  localisations. The \emph{relative {\v C}ech complex} \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet
157	  (\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) for \(f : X \to S\) is the analogous complex in
158	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) obtained by applying \(f_*\) over each finite
159	  intersection instead of global sections; its terms are
160	  \(\prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (f|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})_*
161	  \bigl(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}\bigr)\), and it is a complex in
162	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) because each \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine and the
163	  pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated
164	  morphism is quasi-coherent.
165	\end{definition}
166	
167	\subsection{The three-part construction of the relative {\v C}ech complex}
168	\label{sec:cech_three_part}
169	
170	It is convenient to factor the construction of
171	Definitions~\ref{def:cech_nerve}--\ref{def:cech_complex} into three independent
172	pieces. Two of them are purely formal and unconditional; the sole non-trivial
173	mathematical content is isolated in the middle piece.
174	
175	\paragraph{(1) Geometric backbone — the augmented {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow.}
176	Package the affine cover as a single morphism
177	\(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) (the canonical map out of the
178	coproduct induced by the inclusions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\)). The augmented
179	{\v C}ech nerve of this arrow is an augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
180	\(X\): its object in simplicial degree \(p\) is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
181	\[
182	  \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
183	    \;=\;
184	    \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
185	      U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
186	\]
187	with the augmentation the cover map to \(X\). Because the \(U_i\) are open
188	subschemes of \(X\), each fibre product \(U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p}\)
189	is canonically the intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\). This backbone exists
190	\emph{unconditionally}: it uses only that the category of schemes has coproducts
191	and finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the {\v C}ech nerve of an
192	arrow). It carries no sheaf-theoretic data yet — it is the simplicial diagram of
193	spaces over which the construction is indexed.
194	
195	\paragraph{(2) Push--pull functor — lifting the backbone to modules.}
196	The geometric backbone is turned into a cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module by
197	the \emph{relative-direct-image functor on the over-category}
198	\[
199	  G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
200	  \qquad
201	  (Y \xrightarrow{\,p\,} X) \;\longmapsto\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
202	\]
203	sending an \(X\)-scheme \(p : Y \to X\) to the pushforward along \(p\) of the
204	pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\). Composing \(G\) with the (opposite of the) simplicial
205	backbone of (1) produces exactly the augmented cosimplicial object of
206	Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: on the degree-\(p\) piece
207	\(p : U_{i_0 \ldots i_p} \hookrightarrow X\) one has
208	\(p_* p^*\mathcal{F} = (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\),
209	and a morphism of \(X\)-schemes \(h : (Y_1, p_1) \to (Y_2, p_2)\) (so
210	\(h \circ p_2 = p_1\)\,, reading \(p\) as the structure map) induces the
211	restriction comparison \(G(p_2) \to G(p_1)\) via the unit/counit of the
212	pull-push adjunction together with the over-triangle identifying the two
213	structure maps. This functor \(G\) is the \emph{one non-formal step} in the whole
214	construction. Its functoriality --- that \(G\) respects identities and composition
215	of morphisms over \(X\) --- is precisely a statement about the coherence of the
216	comparison isomorphisms \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) and
217	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\) for pushforward and pullback of sheaves of
218	modules, together with their compatibility with the adjunction units. Concretely,
219	pullback and pushforward of sheaves of modules are organised as a
220	pseudofunctor \(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to
221	\mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\) (sending a morphism to the adjoint pair
222	\(p^* \dashv p_*\)), and the comparison isomorphisms
223	\((p \circ q)^* \cong q^* p^*\), \((p \circ q)_* \cong p_* q_*\) are its structure
224	\(2\)-cells. The functoriality of \(G\) needs exactly three facts: the mate identity
225	expressing the conjugate of the inverse pullback comparison as the pushforward
226	comparison, its normalisation at the identity morphism, and the pseudofunctor
227	unitor and pentagon (associativity) coherences. The laws are nonetheless not formal
228	trivialities: each is a multi-step mate calculation that transports the comparison
229	isomorphisms along the over-triangle \(g \circ p_2 = p_1\) of structure maps and
230	past the adjunction unit, transporting along the over-triangle equality; the
231	obstacle is the bookkeeping of those transports, not a missing geometric ingredient.
232	
233	\paragraph{(3) Coherence-free plumbing — from the nerve to the cochain complex.}
234	The passage from the augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module of (1)--(2)
235	to the relative {\v C}ech complex in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) is again purely
236	formal and uses \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence. One forgets the
237	augmentation, pushes the cosimplicial object forward along \(f : X \to S\) (a
238	covariant operation applied degreewise), and takes the alternating-coface-map
239	cochain complex of the resulting cosimplicial object of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules.
240	This step relies only on the preadditivity of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\): the
241	alternating sum \(\sum_{j} (-1)^j d^j\) of the cofaces is defined in any
242	preadditive category and squares to zero by the cosimplicial identities. Thus the
243	relative {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\)
244	of Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} is genuinely \emph{defined} in terms of the
245	{\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}: once the nerve is constructed,
246	the complex follows with no further input. In summary, of the three pieces only
247	the push--pull functor (2) requires content beyond formal category theory, and
248	that content is the pseudofunctor coherence of pushforward and pullback: the
249	comparison \(2\)-cells of the pseudofunctor
250	\(\mathrm{LocallyDiscrete}(\mathbf{Sch}^{\mathrm{op}}) \to \mathbf{Adj}\,\mathbf{Cat}\)
251	and their unitor/pentagon identities.
252	
253	\paragraph{Formal bricks of the three-part construction.}
254	The geometric backbone (1), the push--pull functor (2), and the plumbing (3) are
255	recorded as the following declarations. The backbone and plumbing are
256	unconditional; the push--pull object and morphism maps are likewise unconditional
257	bricks, and the functor laws assembling them are the lone coherence statement.
258	
259	\begin{definition}\leanok
260	[Arrow of an affine cover]
261	  \label{def:cover_arrow}
262	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrow}
263	  Let \(\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be an affine open cover. Its
264	  \emph{cover arrow} is the single morphism
265	  \(\coprod_{i \in I} U_i \longrightarrow X\) obtained as the universal map out of
266	  the coproduct induced by the open immersions \(U_i \hookrightarrow X\). Packaging
267	  the cover as one arrow lets the generic {\v C}ech-nerve-of-an-arrow machinery
268	  apply.
269	\end{definition}
270	
271	\begin{definition}[Augmented {\v C}ech nerve of a cover]
272	  \label{def:cover_cech_nerve}
273	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerve, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOver, AlgebraicGeometry.coverCechNerveOverAug}
274	  \uses{def:cover_arrow}
275	  The augmented {\v C}ech nerve of the cover arrow of
276	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_arrow}: the augmented \emph{simplicial scheme} over
277	  \(X\) whose degree-\(p\) object is the \((p+1)\)-fold fibre power
278	  \[
279	    \Bigl(\coprod_{i} U_i\Bigr)^{\times_X (p+1)}
280	      = \coprod_{(i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}}
281	        U_{i_0} \times_X \cdots \times_X U_{i_p},
282	  \]
283	  with augmentation the cover map to \(X\). It exists unconditionally because the
284	  category of schemes has all finite limits (hence the wide pullbacks underlying the
285	  {\v C}ech nerve of an arrow). This is the geometric backbone (1).
286	\end{definition}
287	
288	\begin{definition}\leanok
289	[Push--pull object map]
290	  \label{def:push_pull_obj}
291	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullObj}
292	  The object map of the push--pull functor \(G\): to an \(X\)-scheme
293	  \(p : Y \to X\) it assigns
294	  \[
295	    G(Y, p) \;=\; p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F},
296	  \]
297	  the pushforward along \(p\) of the pullback \(p^*\mathcal{F}\).
298	\end{definition}
299	
300	\begin{definition}[Push--pull comparison map]
301	  \label{def:push_pull_map}
302	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_eq_raw, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_self_gen, AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardComp_hom_app_id, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_comp}
303	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj}
304	  The morphism map of \(G\). A morphism \(g : (Y_2, p_2) \to (Y_1, p_1)\) of
305	  \(X\)-schemes has underlying map \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) satisfying the
306	  over-triangle \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\); the contravariant functor produces the
307	  restriction comparison \(G(Y_1, p_1) \to G(Y_2, p_2)\) as the five-step composite
308	  \[
309	    p_{1*}\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
310	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{1*}(\eta)\,}
311	    p_{1*}\,\bar g_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
312	      \xrightarrow{\,\sim\,}
313	    (p_1 \circ \bar g)_*\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
314	      \;=\;
315	    p_{2*}\,\bar g^*\,p_1^*\mathcal{F}
316	      \xrightarrow{\,p_{2*}(\sim)\,}
317	    p_{2*}\,p_2^*\mathcal{F},
318	  \]
319	  where \(\eta\) is the unit of the adjunction \(\bar g^* \dashv \bar g_*\), the
320	  second arrow is the pushforward comparison \(p_{1*}\bar g_* \cong (p_1 \bar g)_*\),
321	  the equality is the substitution \(p_2 = p_1 \circ \bar g\) of the over-triangle,
322	  and the last arrow applies \(p_{2*}\) to the pullback comparison
323	  \(\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (p_1 \bar g)^* = p_2^*\). The two over-triangle
324	  substitutions are realised as coercions along \(p_1 \circ \bar g = p_2\).
325	\end{definition}
326	
327	\begin{lemma}
328	[Push--pull functor — identity law]
329	  \label{lem:push_pull_id}
330	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_id}
331	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map}
332	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} respects identities:
333	  \(G(\mathrm{id}_Y) = \mathrm{id}_{G(Y)}\) for every \(X\)-scheme \(Y\).
334	\end{lemma}
335	
336	\begin{proof}
337	  A direct calculation with comparison isomorphisms, with no sectionwise content.
338	  Rewrite the head \(p_*(\eta)\) followed by the pushforward comparison as the
339	  conjugate-mate of the inverse pullback comparison; the identity leg then collapses
340	  by the identity normalisation of the pseudofunctor together with right unitality,
341	  and the two over-triangle transport coercions cancel against the unitality coercion.
342	\end{proof}
343	
344	\begin{lemma}
345	[Push--pull functor — composition law]
346	  \label{lem:push_pull_comp}
347	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.rawPushPullMap_comp, AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_pentagon}
348	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
349	  The morphism map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} is contravariantly functorial:
350	  for composable morphisms \(h : Y_3 \to Y_2\), \(g : Y_2 \to Y_1\) of \(X\)-schemes,
351	  \[
352	    G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g).
353	  \]
354	  Together with Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} this assembles
355	  Definitions~\ref{def:push_pull_obj}--\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a functor
356	  \(G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \to \operatorname{QCoh}(X)\); composing \(G\) with
357	  the backbone of Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} yields the {\v C}ech nerve of
358	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}.
359	\end{lemma}
360	
361	\begin{proof}
362	  \uses{lem:push_pull_unit_mate, lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
363	  The composition identity \(G(g \circ h) = G(h) \circ G(g)\) follows from three
364	  coherence facts. First, the composite adjunction unit \(\eta^{g \circ h}\) decomposes into
365	  the iterated units \(\eta^{g}\), \(\eta^{h}\) via the mate identity of
366	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_unit_mate} and the naturality of the adjunction unit. Second,
367	  the resulting pullback-side identity is the \emph{pentagon coherence} of the pullback
368	  pseudofunctor — the associativity \(2\)-cell relating the two bracketings of the comparison
369	  isomorphism \((p \circ q \circ r)^* \cong r^* q^* p^*\). Third, the over-triangle transport
370	  coherences are absorbed by Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}.
371	\end{proof}
372	
373	\begin{lemma}
374	[Base-change unit (mate) identity for the push--pull head]
375	  \label{lem:push_pull_unit_mate}
376	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_unit_mate}
377	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
378	  For composable scheme morphisms \(f : A \to B\), \(p : B \to Z\) and a
379	  \(\mathcal{O}_Z\)-module \(N\), the adjunction unit \(\eta^p_N\) of
380	  \(p^* \dashv p_*\) at \(N\), followed by \(p_*\) of the unit \(\eta^f\) for
381	  \(f^* \dashv f_*\) at \(p^*N\), followed by the pushforward comparison
382	  \(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\), equals the unit \(\eta^{f \circ p}_N\) for the
383	  composite followed by \((f \circ p)_*\) applied to the inverse pullback
384	  comparison \((f \circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\):
385	  \[
386	    \eta^p_N \;\circ\; p_*(\eta^f_{p^*N}) \;\circ\;
387	      \bigl(p_* f_* \cong (f \circ p)_*\bigr)
388	    \;=\;
389	    \eta^{f \circ p}_N \;\circ\; (f \circ p)_*\bigl((f\circ p)^* \cong f^* p^*\bigr)^{-1}.
390	  \]
391	  This is the mate-calculus core that converts the single-morphism unit
392	  \(\eta^{f \circ p}\) into the iterated units \(\eta^p, \eta^f\); it is the
393	  reusable ingredient consumed by the functoriality (pentagon) law of the
394	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map}.
395	\end{lemma}
396	
397	\begin{proof}
398	  A direct mate-calculus computation: unfold both sides using the definition of
399	  the adjunction unit for the composite and the pseudofunctor structure maps, then
400	  verify the equality by the mate identity for the pushforward/pullback comparison.
401	\end{proof}
402	
403	\begin{lemma}
404	[Over-triangle transport cancellation for the push--pull tail]
405	  \label{lem:push_pull_transport_cancel}
406	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPull_transport_cancel}
407	  \uses{def:push_pull_map}
408	  Let \(\bar g : Y_2 \to Y_1\), \(p_1 : Y_1 \to X\), \(p_2 : Y_2 \to X\) satisfy the
409	  over-triangle \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\), and let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a
410	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the pullback-comparison leg of the push--pull map,
411	  conjugated by the two \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions along the over-triangle,
412	  collapses to the transport-light form
413	  \[
414	    e_1 \;\circ\; p_{2*}\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
415	      \;\circ\; e_2
416	    \;=\;
417	    (\bar g p_1)_*\bigl((\bar g^* p_1^* \cong (\bar g p_1)^*)_{\mathcal{F}}\bigr)
418	      \;\circ\; e_3,
419	  \]
420	  where \(e_1, e_2, e_3\) are the \(\mathrm{eqToHom}\) coercions induced by
421	  \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\). It states the over-triangle cancellation in the
422	  push--pull comparison map of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} with the
423	  over-triangle equality as a free hypothesis, so a single substitution turns the
424	  transports into identities; it is the reusable pre-coherence brick for the
425	  composition (pentagon) law.
426	\end{lemma}
427	
428	\begin{proof}
429	  Substitute the over-triangle equality \(\bar g \circ p_1 = p_2\) to replace
430	  transport coercions by identities, then verify the cancellation by direct
431	  calculation with the pushforward comparison.
432	\end{proof}
433	
434	\begin{definition}
435	[Push--pull functor]
436	  \label{def:push_pull_functor}
437	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pushPullFunctor}
438	  \uses{def:push_pull_obj, def:push_pull_map, lem:push_pull_id, lem:push_pull_comp}
439	  The \emph{push--pull functor}
440	  \[
441	    G : (X/\mathbf{Sch})^{\mathrm{op}} \longrightarrow \operatorname{QCoh}(X),
442	  \]
443	  assembling the object map \(p \mapsto p_*\, p^*\mathcal{F}\) of
444	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_obj} and the comparison morphism map of
445	  Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_map} into a genuine functor. Functoriality is exactly the two
446	  laws established above: the identity law
447	  Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_id} and the composition law Lemma~\ref{lem:push_pull_comp}. The
448	  functor is contravariant in the over-category \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\); it is the lift of the
449	  geometric backbone (1) to \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules described as step (2) above.
450	\end{definition}
451	
452	\begin{definition}
453	[{\v C}ech nerve cosimplicial object]
454	  \label{def:cech_nerve_cosimplicial}
455	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechNerveCosimplicial}
456	  \uses{def:cover_cech_nerve, def:push_pull_functor}
457	  The (non-augmented) cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module obtained by transporting the
458	  geometric {\v C}ech backbone in \((X/\mathbf{Sch})\) of
459	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_cech_nerve} through the push--pull functor
460	  \(G\) of Definition~\ref{def:push_pull_functor}: degreewise, the \(p\)-th term is the
461	  product over \((i_0, \ldots, i_p) \in I^{p+1}\) of the \((p+1)\)-fold-intersection
462	  pushforwards \((j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\). This is the
463	  underlying cosimplicial object that the {\v C}ech nerve of
464	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} augments by the structure map \(\mathcal{F} \to
465	  \mathcal{C}^0\).
466	\end{definition}
467	
468	\begin{definition}\leanok
469	[Relative {\v C}ech complex from a nerve]
470	  \label{def:relative_cech_complex_of_nerve}
471	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.relativeCechComplexOfNerve}
472	  \uses{def:cech_nerve}
473	  Given \(f : X \to S\) and an augmented cosimplicial \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
474	  \(N\), the \emph{relative {\v C}ech cochain complex} in \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)
475	  obtained by forgetting the augmentation of \(N\), pushing the resulting
476	  cosimplicial object forward degreewise along \(f\), and taking the
477	  alternating-coface-map cochain complex. This passage uses only the preadditivity
478	  of \(\operatorname{QCoh}(S)\) and \emph{no} pushforward/pullback coherence; it is
479	  the coherence-free plumbing (3) that turns the nerve of
480	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve} into the {\v C}ech complex.
481	\end{definition}

483	\section{Affine acyclicity of the {\v C}ech complex}
484	
485	\begin{definition}
486	[Standard affine cover from a spanning family]
487	  \label{def:standard_affine_cover}
488	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}
489	  \mathlibok
490	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib (\texttt{Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open}).}
491	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring (so \(R = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R,
492	  \mathcal{O})\)) and let \(s : \iota \to R\) be a family of elements that generate
493	  the unit ideal, \(\operatorname{Ideal.span}(\operatorname{range} s) = \top\). The
494	  \emph{standard affine open cover} of \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) associated with
495	  \(s\) is the affine open cover whose index set is \(\iota\), whose \(i\)-th member
496	  is the basic open \(D(s_i)\), and whose \(i\)-th structure morphism is the map of
497	  affine schemes induced by the localisation homomorphism \(R \to R_{s_i}\); that
498	  is, in Mathlib notation the defining equation
499	  \(\bigl(\text{affineOpenCoverOfSpanRangeEqTop}\bigr)_f\, i =
500	  \operatorname{Spec.map}\bigl(\text{algebraMap}\; R\; (\operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i))\bigr)\),
501	  so that the sections over the \(i\)-th piece are the \emph{away} localisation
502	  \(R_{s_i} = \operatorname{Localization.Away}(s_i)\) and the geometric piece is
503	  \(D(s_i)\). This is exactly the standard open covering
504	  \(\operatorname{Spec} R = \bigcup_i D(s_i)\) by basic opens, packaged as a single
505	  cover object. It is the spanning-family bundle \((s, hs)\) that the affine
506	  {\v C}ech vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is stated over.
507	\end{definition}
508	
509	\subsection{The categorical--module bridge (L1)}
510	\label{sec:cech_l1_bridge}
511	
512	The affine vanishing of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} is a statement about the
513	\emph{section} {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
514	\mathcal{F})\) of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} --- a cochain complex of
515	abelian groups --- whereas the combinatorial contracting-homotopy core operates on a
516	concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules. The passage between the two, the
517	\emph{categorical--module bridge}, decomposes into two self-contained statements: the
518	section identification (each section group is an away localisation, with the
519	restriction maps the canonical localisation maps) and the homology-to-exactness
520	reduction (vanishing of the abstract homology is equivalent to exactness of the
521	underlying localised-module complex). These are isolated as
522	Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized} and
523	Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} below; the assembly --- feeding each
524	positive-degree node through the local-to-global exactness criterion and closing with
525	the dependent combinatorial port --- is carried out in the proof of
526	Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}.
527	
528	\begin{definition}
529	
530	[Quasi-coherent sections over a basic open are an away localisation]
531	  \label{def:qcoh_sections_localized}

542	  % on $\mathcal{O}_{\Spec(R)}$ and $\widetilde M$

546	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Schemes, \texttt{lemma-spec-sheaves} (Tag 01HV),
547	  items (4)--(5).}
548	  Let \(R\) be a commutative ring, \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
549	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec} R}\)-module, and write
550	  \(M = \Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})\) for its module of global
551	  sections. For every \(g \in R\), the restriction-of-sections map
552	  \(M = \mathcal{F}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g))\) exhibits the
553	  section group \(\mathcal{F}(D(g))\) as the away localisation \(M_g\) of \(M\); that
554	  is, this map is an \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}\) for the multiplicative
555	  submonoid \(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g\) of powers of \(g\). Moreover, for
556	  \(D(g_2) \subseteq D(g_1)\) the restriction map
557	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(g_1)) \to \mathcal{F}(D(g_2))\) is, under these identifications, the
558	  canonical localisation map \(M_{g_1} \to M_{g_2}\) (the unique \(R\)-linear map
559	  compatible with the localisation units). Item~(4) of Tag~01HV gives the first
560	  assertion for \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) (\(\Gamma(D(g), \widetilde{M}) = M_g\)
561	  as an \(R_g\)-module) and item~(5) gives the second (the restriction along
562	  \(D(g) \subset D(f)\) is the localisation map \(M_f \to M_g\)).
563	
564	  The module-level input on the side of Mathlib is the development
565	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Tilde}: the comparison map
566	  \((\operatorname{tilde.toOpen} M\, (D(g))).\mathrm{hom}\) carries the instance
567	  \(\operatorname{IsLocalizedModule}(\operatorname{Submonoid.powers} g)\), and
568	  \(\operatorname{isUnit\_algebraMap\_end\_basicOpen}\) records that \(g\) acts
569	  invertibly on the sections over \(D(g)\); the restriction compatibility is
570	  \(\operatorname{tilde.toOpen\_res}\). These supply the statement \emph{verbatim} for
571	  the standard sheaf \(\widetilde{M}\). For an \emph{arbitrary} quasi-coherent
572	  \(\mathcal{F}\) the remaining step is the canonical comparison
573	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma(\operatorname{Spec} R, \mathcal{F})}\), i.e.\
574	  that the counit \(\operatorname{Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ}\) is an isomorphism. This
575	  is \emph{not} yet available in Mathlib for a general quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\):
576	  Mathlib provides it only from a \emph{global} presentation
577	  (\(\operatorname{isIso\_fromTildeΓ\_of\_presentation}\)), whereas quasi-coherence
578	  supplies only \emph{local} presentation data on a cover. Globalising the local data
579	  on the affine \(\operatorname{Spec} R\) --- the affine equivalence
580	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(\operatorname{Spec} R) \simeq \operatorname{Mod}(R)\), Stacks
581	  Tag~01I8 --- is the one genuine gap this definition records as a build target; it is
582	  the canonical missing affine equivalence and is self-contained.
583	
584	  Two combinatorial--geometric ingredients package the identification into the form
585	  consumed by Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}. First, the multi-index
586	  intersection identity
587	  \(\bigl(\bigcap_k D(s_{\sigma(k)})\bigr) = D\bigl(\prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\bigr)\) in
588	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R).\mathrm{Opens}\) (\(\operatorname{basicOpen\_sprod}\), proved
589	  by induction on the index length using the infimum-splitting helper
590	  \(\operatorname{iInf\_fin\_succ}\) and \(\operatorname{PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen\_mul}\)):
591	  it rewrites the \((p+1)\)-fold intersection over a multi-index \(\sigma\) as the single
592	  basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\), so the
593	  section group there is the away localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). Second, the restriction
594	  compatibility of item~(5) is made explicit: for a basic-open inclusion
595	  \(D(b) \subseteq D(a)\) the presheaf restriction map of \(\widetilde{M}\) between the
596	  two section groups \emph{is} the away-localisation comparison
597	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison}\) of the two localisation units
598	  (\(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\), by the uniqueness
599	  \(\operatorname{AwayComparison.comparison\_unique}\) of a map of localised modules);
600	  this is the single differential brick that the {\v C}ech coface match of
601	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact} feeds on.
602	\end{definition}

633	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
634	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (the {\v C}ech complex is

636	  % L58--67).
637	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Clearly the {\v C}ech complex
638	  % $\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})$
639	  % is identified with the complex
640	  % $$
641	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0} M_{f_{i_0}} \to
642	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}} \to
643	  % \prod\nolimits_{i_0i_1i_2} M_{f_{i_0}f_{i_1}f_{i_2}} \to

646	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
647	  \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial} (identification with the
648	  complex of localisations).}
649	  Let \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_{\operatorname{Spec}
650	  R}\)-module and \(\mathcal{U}\) the standard cover associated with a spanning family
651	  \(s : \iota \to R\). Under the section identification of
652	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}, the section {\v C}ech complex
653	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
654	  Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex} is isomorphic, as a cochain complex, to
655	  the concrete complex of localised \(R\)-modules
656	  \[
657	    D^\bullet : \quad
658	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
659	    \prod_{\sigma : \{0,1\} \to \iota} M_{s_\sigma} \;\longrightarrow\;
660	    \cdots,
661	    \qquad
662	    s_\sigma = \prod_{k} s_{\sigma(k)},
663	  \]
664	  whose differential is the alternating sum of the canonical localisation maps. The
665	  intended top-level conclusion is the directed homology vanishing
666	  \[
667	    1 \leq p
668	    \;\Longrightarrow\;
669	    \operatorname{IsZero}\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
670	      \mathcal{F}).\mathrm{homology}\, p\bigr),
671	  \]
672	  which is the form the affine vanishing
673	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine} (\(\operatorname{sectionCech\_affine\_vanishing}\))
674	  consumes; it is read off from positive-degree exactness of the underlying group
675	  homomorphisms,
676	  \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}\bigl(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to D^{p+1}\bigr)\), which is
677	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}.
678	
679	  The reduction from the abstract homology to this group-level exactness is \emph{not}
680	  a \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\) statement: the degree-\(p\) object of
681	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F})\) is the \emph{categorical
682	  product}
683	  \(\prod^{c}_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \iota}
684	  \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) in the category \(\mathrm{Ab}\) of abelian groups (the
685	  alternating-coface-map complex of the \(\mathrm{Ab}\)-valued cosimplicial object
686	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechCosimplicial}\)), not an \(R\)-module object, so the
687	  module-category criterion \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.moduleCat\_exact\_iff}\) does
688	  not apply. The correct chain runs through three steps, isolated as the sub-lemmas
689	  below: \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology is \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) at
690	  \(p\) (\(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\)); \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\)
691	  of a short complex in \(\mathrm{Ab}\) is the underlying-group exactness
692	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\) via the abelian
693	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
694	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}); and the categorical product is moved to the
695	  per-\(\sigma\) localised modules by the element-level product equivalence
696	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
697	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\)
698	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}), under which the {\v C}ech coface
699	  differential matches the localisation differential \(\operatorname{dDiff}\) of
700	  \(D^\bullet\) (the abstract cosimplicial unfold
701	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_objd_apply} followed by the tilde-bridge identification
702	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). Transporting the
703	  already-proved exactness \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\) of
704	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact} across the product equivalence then
705	  discharges the group-level exactness. The active target is the tilde case
706	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\), for which the per-coordinate comparisons are
707	  immediate from \(\operatorname{qcohRestriction\_eq\_comparison}\); a general
708	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{F}\) is reduced to this case via the affine equivalence
709	  \(\mathcal{F} \cong \widetilde{\Gamma\mathcal{F}}\) (Stacks Tag~01I8), the
710	  separately-deferred globalisation gap recorded in
711	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}.
712	\end{lemma}
713	
714	\begin{proof}
715	  \uses{def:qcoh_sections_localized, def:section_cech_complex}
716	  The term identification is the degreewise statement of
717	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: the degree-\(p\) section group
718	  \(\prod_\sigma \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is \(\prod_\sigma M_{s_\sigma}\), since
719	  each \((p+1)\)-fold intersection \(D(s_{\sigma(0)}) \cap \cdots \cap
720	  D(s_{\sigma(p)})\) is the basic open \(D(s_\sigma)\) of the product
721	  \(s_\sigma = \prod_k s_{\sigma(k)}\) and the sections there are the away
722	  localisation \(M_{s_\sigma}\). The differential identification is item~(5) of
723	  Definition~\ref{def:qcoh_sections_localized}: each face restriction
724	  \(\mathcal{F}(D(s_{\sigma \circ d_j})) \to \mathcal{F}(D(s_\sigma))\) is the
725	  canonical localisation map \(M_{s_{\sigma \circ d_j}} \to M_{s_\sigma}\), so the
726	  alternating sum of restrictions is the alternating sum of localisation maps, the
727	  differential of \(D^\bullet\) (the coface match of
728	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_coface_match}). The homology vanishing then follows in
729	  three steps. First, \(\operatorname{exactAt\_iff\_isZero\_homology}\) turns
730	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of the homology at \(p\) into \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) of
731	  the short complex at \(p\). Second, because the degree-\(p\) objects are
732	  \emph{categorical products in \(\mathrm{Ab}\)} rather than \(R\)-modules, the abelian
733	  analogue \(\operatorname{ShortComplex.ab\_exact\_iff}\)
734	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_ab_exact}) --- not the module-category criterion ---
735	  reduces \(\operatorname{ExactAt}\) to the underlying-group exactness
736	  \(\forall x_2,\; g(x_2) = 0 \Rightarrow \exists x_1,\; f(x_1) = x_2\). Third, the
737	  element-level product equivalence
738	  \(\operatorname{ToType}(\prod^{c} F_\sigma) \simeq \prod_\sigma
739	  \operatorname{ToType}(F_\sigma)\) of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_product_equiv}
740	  identifies each product group with the per-\(\sigma\) localised module \(D^p\), so the
741	  group-level exactness is exactly \(\operatorname{Function.Exact}(D^{p-1} \to D^p \to
742	  D^{p+1})\), supplied by \(\operatorname{dDiff\_exact}\)
743	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_module_exact}).
744	
745	  The identification of the abstract section complex with the concrete localised
746	  complex \(D^\bullet\) --- together with the construction of the prepend transport
747	  \(c\) and the localisation cofaces \(\delta_k\) discharging the unit, shift, and
748	  coface-commutation compatibilities \(\mathtt{hu}\)/\(\mathtt{hsh}\)/\(\mathtt{hcomm}\)
749	  of the dependent combinatorial port --- is the bulk of the bridge. The non-circular
750	  route is reflection along the localisation equivalence on its essential image (the
751	  global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is the inverse equivalence there, so it reflects
752	  \(\operatorname{IsZero}\) of homology); it is \emph{not} the assertion that
753	  ``\(\Gamma\) preserves homology'', which would presuppose the very affine exactness
754	  being proved.
755	\end{proof}

1650	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1651	  (sectionwise reduction).}
1652	  Let \(R\) be a presheaf of rings on a category \(C\) and let
1653	  \(\varphi : K \to L\) be a morphism of chain complexes in the abelian category
1654	  \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) of presheaves of \(R\)-modules. Suppose that for \emph{every}
1655	  object \(V\) the evaluated morphism — the image of \(\varphi\) under the functor
1656	  \(\bigl(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\bigr).
1657	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\), a morphism of complexes of
1658	  \(R(V)\)-modules — is a quasi-isomorphism. Then \(\varphi\) is a
1659	  quasi-isomorphism. This is the objectwise-reduction step that turns the
1660	  resolution claim of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} into a family of
1661	  sectionwise (combinatorial) statements: homology in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) is
1662	  computed open-by-open, because the evaluation functors
1663	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) are exact (hence preserve
1664	  homology) and jointly conservative. The Lean entry point is
1665	  \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\), which combines the joint
1666	  conservativity of the evaluations (each \(g\) is an isomorphism as soon as every
1667	  \((\operatorname{evaluation} R\, V).\operatorname{map} g\) is) with the fact that
1668	  each \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation} R\, V\) preserves homology.
1669	\end{lemma}
1670	
1671	\begin{proof}
1672	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
1673	  A morphism of complexes is a quasi-isomorphism iff it induces an isomorphism on
1674	  homology in every degree (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\),
1675	  \(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_iff\_isIso\_homologyMap}\)). Fix a degree \(i\). The
1676	  homology map \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism in \(\mathrm{PMod}(R)\) iff each of
1677	  its evaluations is an isomorphism, since the underlying
1678	  presheaf-of-abelian-groups functor reflects isomorphisms and a morphism of
1679	  presheaves is an isomorphism iff it is so objectwise (joint conservativity). For
1680	  each \(V\), evaluation preserves homology, so the evaluation of \(H_i(\varphi)\)
1681	  is the homology map of the evaluated complex morphism; the latter is an
1682	  isomorphism because the evaluated morphism is a quasi-isomorphism by hypothesis.
1683	  Hence \(H_i(\varphi)\) is an isomorphism for all \(i\), i.e.\ \(\varphi\) is a
1684	  quasi-isomorphism.
1685	\end{proof}

1707	  for the set of cover members containing \(V\). Evaluating the free presheaf complex

1713	        \operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma)(V)
1714	      = \bigoplus_{\substack{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I \\ V \leq U_\sigma}}
1715	        \mathcal{O}_X(V)
1716	      = \bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V),

1719	  \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise (poset hom-set is a subsingleton), and

1722	  differential of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) is the alternating-sum combinatorial
1723	  {\v C}ech differential of the full simplex on the index set \(I_1\) with

1749	  \uses{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex, def:cover_structure_presheaf}
1750	  The degreewise identity is the value at \(V\) of the explicit description of the
1751	  free presheaf \(\operatorname{free}(\mathbf{y}\,W)\) recorded in
1752	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}: it sends \(V\) to the free
1753	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module on \(\operatorname{Hom}(V, W)\), which is
1754	  \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) if \(V \leq W\) and \(0\) otherwise. Applying
1755	  \(\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\;V\) to the coproduct decomposition
1756	  \(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex\_X}\) commutes with the direct sum and
1757	  collapses each summand to \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) or \(0\) according to whether
1758	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\), i.e.\ whether \(\sigma\) factors through \(I_1\); the surviving
1759	  summands are exactly those indexed by \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The
1760	  evaluated faces are the index-dropping restriction maps, which on the surviving
1761	  summands are identities of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), so the alternating sum is the
1762	  combinatorial {\v C}ech differential. The augmentation value follows from
1763	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) exactly when some \(U_i\)
1764	  contains \(V\) (\(I_1 \neq \varnothing\)), recorded in

1772	\begin{lemma}
1773	[Constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech contracting-homotopy engine]
1774	  \label{lem:free_cech_engine}
1775	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential,
1776	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_comp,
1777	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_eq_of_cocycle,
1778	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combDifferential_exact,
1779	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy,
1780	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_spec,
1781	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy_zero,
1782	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.combSign_flip,
1783	    AlgebraicGeometry.FreeCechEngine.cons_comp_succAbove_succ,
1784	    AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_sigma_of_forall_isZero}
1785	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1786	  % (proof, the differential and contracting map of $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$) (read from
1787	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1245--1268).
1788	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1789	  % is given by
1790	  % $$
1791	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1792	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1793	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1794	  % $$
1795	  % The sum is finite as the element $s$ has finite support.
1796	  % Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1797	  % $$
1798	  % h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)
1799	  % $$
1800	  % by the rule
1801	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1802	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1803	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1804	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1805	  % \end{matrix} \right.$"
1806	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1807	  (differential and contracting homotopy of the evaluated complex).}
1808	  Fix a nonempty index set \(J\) (in the application \(J = I_1(V)\)), a chosen index
1809	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in J\), and a single coefficient module \(M\). The
1810	  \emph{constant-coefficient combinatorial {\v C}ech engine} consists of: the
1811	  alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\) on the
1812	  full simplex over \(J\) with coefficients \(M\),
1813	  \[
1814	    (\operatorname{combDifferential} t)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1815	      = \sum_{j=0}^{p+1} (-1)^j\, t_{i_0 \ldots \widehat{i_j} \ldots i_{p+1}},
1816	  \]
1817	  which squares to zero, \(\operatorname{combDifferential} \circ
1818	  \operatorname{combDifferential} = 0\) (\(\operatorname{combDifferential\_comp}\)); the
1819	  \emph{prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)} homotopy \(\operatorname{combHomotopy}\),
1820	  \((\operatorname{combHomotopy}\, i_{\mathrm{fix}}\, u)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}}
1821	  = u_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}}\) when \(i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) and \(0\) otherwise; and
1822	  the contracting identity \(d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}\)
1823	  (\(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_spec}\)), which yields positive-degree exactness
1824	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_exact}\) and the cocycle-splitting form
1825	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential\_eq\_of\_cocycle}\). The remaining declarations are
1826	  sign- and index-bookkeeping consumed by the contracting identity: the
1827	  succ-shift reindexing \(\operatorname{cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) and the
1828	  alternating-sign flip \(\operatorname{combSign\_flip}\), together with the
1829	  degenerate-case simplification \(\operatorname{combHomotopy\_zero}\). The generic
1830	  category-theory helper \(\operatorname{isZero\_sigma\_of\_forall\_isZero}\) (a
1831	  coproduct of zero objects is a zero object) is recorded here as well, used to collapse
1832	  the empty-index direct sums of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}. This engine is
1833	  self-contained combinatorics on \(\operatorname{Fin.cons}\),
1834	  \(\operatorname{Fin.succAbove}\), \(\operatorname{Fin.predAbove}\), and
1835	  \(\operatorname{Finset.sum\_involution}\); it mirrors the contracting homotopy of the
1836	  Stacks \texttt{lemma-homology-complex} proof.
1837	\end{lemma}

1839	\begin{lemma}
1840	[Degreewise identification of the evaluated free complex with the engine complex]
1841	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}
1842	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalEngineIso}
1843	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
1844	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1845	  % (proof, the description of $K^{ext}_p(W)$ and its differential) (read from
1846	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1236--1251).
1847	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
1848	  % $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
1849	  % and
1850	  % $$
1851	  % K^{ext}_p(W) =
1852	  % \bigoplus\nolimits_{i_0 \ldots i_p \in I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(W).
1853	  % $$
1854	  % This is true because of the simple description of the presheaves
1855	  % $(j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}}$.
1856	  % Moreover, the differential of the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$
1857	  % is given by
1858	  % $$
1859	  % d(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} =
1860	  % \sum\nolimits_{j = 0, \ldots, p + 1} \sum\nolimits_{i \in I_1}
1861	  % (-1)^j s_{i_0 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}.
1862	  % $$"
1863	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1864	  (the evaluated complex and its differential).}
1865	  Fix an open \(V\) with \(I_1(V) = \{\, i : V \leq U_i \,\} \neq \varnothing\). There
1866	  is an isomorphism of chain complexes of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules
1867	  \[
1868	    \bigl((\operatorname{eval} V).\operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\bigr)
1869	      \bigl(\operatorname{cechFreePresheafComplex} \mathcal{U}\bigr)
1870	    \;\cong\;
1871	    C_\bullet,
1872	    \qquad
1873	    C_p = \coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V),
1874	  \]
1875	  where the coproduct is taken in \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(\mathcal{O}_X(V))\) and the
1876	  differential of \(C_\bullet\) is the constant-coefficient {\v C}ech differential
1877	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\) on the index set \(J = I_1(V)\)
1878	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}). The isomorphism is \emph{degreewise} and
1879	  \emph{intertwines the evaluated alternating-face differential with}
1880	  \(\operatorname{combDifferential}\). It is the chain/coproduct dual of the section-side
1881	  cochain identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}; isolating it
1882	  here gives the empty and nonempty cases (Lemmas~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}
1883	  and~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}) a common, ready degreewise model.
1884	\end{lemma}
1885	
1886	\begin{proof}
1887	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
1888	  \emph{Degreewise.} By \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_X}\) of
1889	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} evaluation at \(V\) commutes with the
1890	  coproduct,
1891	  \((\operatorname{eval} V)(K(\mathcal{U})_p) \cong \coprod_{\sigma :
1892	  \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \mathcal{U}.I_0} (\operatorname{eval} V)(\operatorname{free}
1893	  (\mathbf{y}\,U_\sigma))\). Split the index set into the \(\sigma\) landing in
1894	  \(I_1(V)\) and the rest. Each summand with \(\sigma\) valued in \(I_1(V)\) (so
1895	  \(V \leq U_\sigma\)) is \(\cong \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) by
1896	  \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_iso\_of\_le}\); each remaining summand is the zero
1897	  object by \(\operatorname{freeYonedaEval\_isZero\_of\_not\_le}\). Hence the coproduct
1898	  collapses to \(\coprod_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1(V)} \mathcal{O}_X(V) =
1899	  C_p\).
1900	
1901	  \emph{Differential match.} The evaluated differential is \((\operatorname{eval} V)\)
1902	  applied to the alternating face sum
1903	  \(\operatorname{AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD}(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}
1904	  \mathcal{U})\). The face \(\delta_i\) reindexes the \(\sigma\)-summand by
1905	  \(\sigma \mapsto \sigma \circ \operatorname{Fin.succAbove} i\) (the face action of
1906	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeSimplicial}\)); restricted to the
1907	  \(I_1\)-summands this is exactly the index-drop that the engine encodes, and the sign
1908	  \((-1)^i\) is supplied by \(\operatorname{objD}\). The match is verified on the
1909	  coproduct injections by \(\operatorname{Limits.Sigma.hom\_ext}\), reusing the
1910	  naturality of \(\operatorname{Limits.PreservesCoproduct.iso}\); summing with signs
1911	  identifies the evaluated differential with
1912	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combDifferential}\). This is the hard content of the
1913	  identification and the chain/coproduct dual of the cochain coface match in
1914	  Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_homology_exact}.
1915	\end{proof}

1917	\begin{lemma}
1918	
1919	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting no cover member]
1920	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_empty}
1921	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_isEmpty,
1922	    AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_isZero_of_isEmpty,
1923	    AlgebraicGeometry.coverStructurePresheaf_eval_isZero_of_isEmpty,
1924	    AlgebraicGeometry.isZero_homology_of_isZero_X}
1925	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine}
1926	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1927	  % (proof, the empty case) (read from references/stacks-cohomology.tex,
1928	  % L1228--1230).
1929	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "If $I_1 = \emptyset$, then the complex $K^{ext}_\bullet(W) = 0$
1930	  % so there is nothing to prove."
1931	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1932	  (empty case).}
1933	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
1934	  \(I_1(V) = \varnothing\), i.e.\ \(V\) is contained in no cover member \(U_i\). Then
1935	  the evaluated augmented complex is identically zero: every degree-\(p\) term
1936	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\) is the empty
1937	  direct sum \(0\) (there is no map \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to \varnothing\)) and the
1938	  augmentation target is \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\). Consequently the
1939	  evaluation at \(V\) of \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a morphism of zero
1940	  complexes, which is a quasi-isomorphism (both sides have vanishing homology). The Lean
1941	  bricks are \(\operatorname{cechFreeEval\_isZero\_of\_isEmpty}\) (each evaluated source
1942	  term is a zero object, via the coproduct-of-zeros helper
1943	  \(\operatorname{isZero\_sigma\_of\_forall\_isZero}\) of
1944	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}),
1945	  \(\operatorname{coverStructurePresheaf\_eval\_isZero\_of\_isEmpty}\) (the evaluated
1946	  augmentation target is zero), and the generic
1947	  \(\operatorname{isZero\_homology\_of\_isZero\_X}\) (a short complex with zero middle
1948	  term has vanishing homology), which together force every
1949	  \(\operatorname{homologyMap}\) to be a map of zero objects.
1950	\end{lemma}
1951	
1952	\begin{proof}
1953	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
1954	  By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} each evaluated term is
1955	  \(\bigoplus_{\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1} \mathcal{O}_X(V)\); when
1956	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\) the index type \(\mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\) is empty, so the
1957	  term is the zero object, and likewise \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = 0\). A
1958	  morphism whose source and target complexes are both zero (so all their homology
1959	  objects are zero, \(\operatorname{IsZero}\)) is a quasi-isomorphism; in Lean this is
1960	  immediate from \(\operatorname{quasiIso\_iff}\) since each
1961	  \(\operatorname{homologyMap}\) is a map of zero objects, hence an isomorphism.
1962	\end{proof}

1968	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:free_cech_engine}
1969	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1970	  % (proof, definition of the contracting map $h$) (read from
1971	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1253--1271).
1972	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
1973	  % $h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)$ by the rule
1974	  % $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} =
1975	  % \left\{ \begin{matrix}
1976	  % 0 & \text{if} & i_0 \not = i_{\text{fix}} \\
1977	  % s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p + 1}} & \text{if} & i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}
1978	  % \end{matrix} \right.$"
1979	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
1980	  (the contracting map \(h\)).}
1981	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
1982	  \(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\) and fix an index \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\). The
1983	  \emph{prepend homotopy} is the family of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-linear maps
1984	  \[
1985	    h_p : K(\mathcal{U})_p(V) \longrightarrow K(\mathcal{U})_{p+1}(V),
1986	    \qquad
1987	    h_p(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p+1}} =
1988	      \begin{cases}
1989	        s_{i_1 \ldots i_{p+1}} & i_0 = i_{\mathrm{fix}}, \\
1990	        0 & i_0 \neq i_{\mathrm{fix}},
1991	      \end{cases}
1992	  \]
1993	  defined on the \(I_1\)-indexed direct sum of
1994	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} as the \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of
1995	  the per-summand prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) maps. This is exactly the
1996	  combinatorial content of \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) of
1997	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}, here with the single
1998	  coefficient module \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), transported across the degreewise
1999	  identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso}. The maps \(h_p\) (together with the
2000	  degree \((-1)\!\to\!0\) map onto \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\))
2001	  are the homotopy hom maps assembled into a
2002	  \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.Homotopy}\) in
2003	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}.

2006	\begin{proof}
2007	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}
2008	  Under the identification of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} each
2009	  evaluated term is a finite direct sum of copies of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\) indexed by
2010	  \(\sigma : \mathrm{Fin}(p+1) \to I_1\). The prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) prescription
2011	  sends the basis component at \((i_1, \ldots, i_{p+1})\) to the component at
2012	  \((i_{\mathrm{fix}}, i_1, \ldots, i_{p+1})\) and is \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-linear; it is
2013	  packaged as the \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\) of these per-summand maps, exactly as
2014	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) is defined. This is a definition,
2015	  so there is nothing to prove beyond well-definedness, which is the linearity of
2016	  \(\operatorname{Sigma.desc}\).

2019	\begin{lemma}
2020	[The prepend homotopy contracts the augmented evaluated complex]
2021	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
2022	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEvalPrependHomotopy_spec}
2023	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine}
2024	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2025	  % (proof, the $dh + hd = \mathrm{id}$ computation) (read from
2026	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1272--1285).
2027	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Then we compute
2028	  % $(dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_0 \ldots i_p} +
2029	  % \sum_{j \geq 1}\sum_{i \in I_1}
2030	  % (-1)^j (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_{j - 1} i i_j \ldots i_p}
2031	  % + (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) d(s)_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
2032	  % which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
2033	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2034	  (the contracting identity).}
2035	  In the situation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
2036	  (\(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\), \(i_{\mathrm{fix}} \in I_1\) fixed), the prepend
2037	  homotopy \(h\) satisfies the contracting identity
2038	  \[
2039	    d \circ h + h \circ d = \mathrm{id}
2040	  \]
2041	  on the augmented evaluated complex
2042	  \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1(V) \to K(\mathcal{U})_0(V) \to
2043	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) \to 0\) (including the degree-\(0\)/degree-\((-1)\)
2044	  augmentation term). Here \(d\) is the alternating-sum {\v C}ech differential of
2045	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}.
2046	\end{lemma}
2047	
2048	\begin{proof}
2049	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine}
2050	  This is the same alternating-sum cancellation as
2051	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\)
2052	  (\(\operatorname{combDifferential}(\operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, t) +
2053	  \operatorname{combHomotopy} r\, (\operatorname{combDifferential} t) = t\)),
2054	  specialised to coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\). Expanding
2055	  \((dh + hd)(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\): the \(j = 0\) term of \(dh\) prepends and then
2056	  removes \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\), returning \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\); every remaining term
2057	  of \(dh\) cancels in pairs against the corresponding term of \(hd\) (the
2058	  prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) of the differential), because removing an index
2059	  \(j \geq 1\) after prepending \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) equals prepending
2060	  \(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) after removing index \(j-1\), with opposite signs. The net
2061	  result is \(s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\), i.e.\ \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\); the index
2062	  bookkeeping is the \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.cons\_comp\_succAbove\_succ}\) /
2063	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combSign\_flip}\) content of
2064	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}.
2065	\end{proof}

2067	\begin{lemma}
2068	[Evaluated free complex over an open meeting a cover member]
2069	  \label{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2070	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty}
2071	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso,
2072	    lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
2073	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Lemma \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2074	  % (proof, homotopy-to-zero of the extended complex) (read from
2075	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L1224--1245).
2076	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
2077	  % $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex."
2078	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}
2079	  (homotopy equivalence to zero).}
2080	  With the notation of Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}, suppose
2081	  \(I_1(V) \neq \varnothing\). Then the evaluation at \(V\) of
2082	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism: the augmented
2083	  evaluated complex is contractible, hence its homology is concentrated in degree
2084	  \(0\) where it equals \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V) = \mathcal{O}_X(V)\).
2085	\end{lemma}
2086	
2087	\begin{proof}
2088	  \uses{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec}
2089	  By Lemmas~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy}
2090	  and~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec} the degreewise prepend maps
2091	  \(h_p\) satisfy \(dh + hd = \mathrm{id}\) on the augmented evaluated complex, so they
2092	  assemble into a \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.Homotopy}\) between the identity
2093	  and the zero endomorphism of that complex. A complex whose identity is homotopic to
2094	  zero is contractible; equivalently the augmented evaluated complex is a
2095	  \(\operatorname{HomotopyEquiv}\) of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet(V)\) with
2096	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V)[0] = \mathcal{O}_X(V)[0]\). A homotopy equivalence is a
2097	  quasi-isomorphism (\(\operatorname{Homotopy.toQuasiIso}\), or
2098	  \(\operatorname{HomotopyEquiv.toQuasiIso}\) /
2099	  \(\operatorname{QuasiIso.ofHomotopyEquiv}\)), so the evaluated augmentation map is a
2100	  quasi-isomorphism.
2101	\end{proof}

2122	  % 0 & \text{if} & i \not = 0 \\
2123	  % \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U} & \text{if} & i = 0
2124	  % \end{matrix}
2125	  % \right.
2126	  % $$"
2127	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2128	  \texttt{lemma-homology-complex}.}
2129	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space and \(\mathcal{U} : U = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) an
2130	  open covering. Let \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) be the cover structure presheaf of
2131	  Definition~\ref{def:cover_structure_presheaf}, the image presheaf of the
2132	  augmentation \(\bigoplus_i (j_i)_{!}\,\mathcal{O}_X|_{U_i} \to \mathcal{O}_X\) (so
2133	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)\) when \(W\) is contained in some
2134	  \(U_i\), and \(0\) otherwise). Then the homology
2135	  presheaves of the free presheaf complex
2136	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex}
2137	  vanish away from degree \(0\) and equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree
2138	  \(0\):
2139	  \[
2140	    H_i\bigl(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\bigr) =
2141	    \begin{cases}
2142	      \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} & i = 0, \\
2143	      0 & i \neq 0.
2144	    \end{cases}
2145	  \]
2146	  Equivalently, the augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to
2147	  K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact as a complex of
2148	  presheaves; that is, \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is a (projective) resolution of
2149	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\), so the augmentation
2150	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\) is a
2151	  quasi-isomorphism — \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) is quasi-isomorphic to
2152	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\).
2153	\end{lemma}
2154	
2155	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Consider the extended complex $K^{ext}_\bullet$ one gets by
2156	% putting
2157	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$ in degree $-1$ with the obvious map
2158	% $K(\mathcal{U})_0 =
2159	% \bigoplus_{i_0} (j_{i_0})_{p!}\mathcal{O}_{U_{i_0}} \to
2160	% \mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}$.
2161	% It suffices to show that taking sections of this extended complex over
2162	% any open $W \subset X$ leads to an acyclic complex.
2163	% In fact, we claim that for every $W \subset X$ the complex
2164	% $K^{ext}_\bullet(W)$ is homotopy equivalent to the zero complex.
2165	% Write $I = I_1 \amalg I_2$ where $W \subset U_i$ if and only
2166	% if $i \in I_1$.
2167	% ...
2168	% If $I_1 \not = \emptyset$, then
2169	% $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}(W) = \mathcal{O}_X(W)$
2170	% ...
2171	% Fix an element $i_{\text{fix}} \in I_1$. Define a map
2172	% $h : K^{ext}_p(W) \longrightarrow K^{ext}_{p + 1}(W)$
2173	% by the rule
2174	% $h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_{p + 1}} = (i_0 = i_{\text{fix}}) s_{i_1 \ldots i_p}$
2175	% ... which is equal to $s_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ as desired."
2176	\begin{proof}
2177	  \uses{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise,
2178	    lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_empty, lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty}
2179	  The claim is that the augmentation chain map
2180	  \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet \to \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\)
2181	  (\(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\)) is a quasi-isomorphism. By the
2182	  objectwise-reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation}
2183	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_of\_evaluation}\)) it suffices to show that for every open
2184	  \(V \subset X\) the evaluation
2185	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{PresheafOfModules.evaluation}\, V).
2186	  \operatorname{mapHomologicalComplex}\bigr)(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug})\) is a
2187	  quasi-isomorphism of complexes of \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-modules.
2188	
2189	  Fix \(V\) and set \(I_1 = I_1(V) = \{\,i : V \leq U_i\,\}\). By
2190	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise} the evaluated augmented complex is the
2191	  augmented combinatorial {\v C}ech complex of the simplex on \(I_1\) with
2192	  coefficients \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\), with augmentation target
2193	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}(V)\). Split into two cases on \(I_1\). If
2194	  \(I_1 = \varnothing\), Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_empty} shows both sides vanish
2195	  and the evaluated augmentation is a quasi-isomorphism. If \(I_1 \neq \varnothing\),
2196	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty} provides the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\)
2197	  contracting homotopy, exhibiting the augmented evaluated complex as contractible and
2198	  hence the evaluated augmentation as a quasi-isomorphism. In either case the
2199	  hypothesis of Lemma~\ref{lem:quasiIso_of_evaluation} holds for \(V\), so
2200	  \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}\) is a quasi-isomorphism; equivalently the
2201	  homology presheaves of \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) vanish away from degree \(0\) and
2202	  equal \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) in degree \(0\).
2203	
2204	  The contracting homotopy is built directly at the \(\mathcal{O}_X(V)\)-module
2205	  (section) level as the prepend-\(i_{\mathrm{fix}}\) map — the same combinatorial
2206	  content as \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy}\) /
2207	  \(\operatorname{FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy\_spec}\) of
2208	  Lemma~\ref{lem:free_cech_engine}.
2209	\end{proof}

2211	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
2212	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
2213	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint}
2214	  \mathlibok
2215	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2216	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Injective.Basic}).}
2217	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
2218	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
2219	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — degreewise engine model of the evaluated free complex

The nonempty-case analysis of the evaluated free Čech complex needs an explicit degreewise
identification of `(evaluation V).obj (K(𝒰)_p)` with the constant-coefficient combinatorial model
`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`, where `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This section builds that
degreewise object isomorphism as the composite

  `(eval V)(K_p) ≅[cechFreeEval_X] ∐_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I₀} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
  `           ≅[drop-zeros]      ∐_{σ : V ≤ U_σ} (eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)`
  `           ≅[whiskerEquiv]    ∐_{τ : Fin(p+1)→I₁(V)} O_X(V),`

using `freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le` to discard the summands with `V ≰ U_σ` and
`freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le` to identify each surviving summand with `O_X(V)`.  The differential match
turning this degreewise iso into an iso of chain complexes is the genuine remaining bottleneck and is
NOT discharged here. -/

variable {X}

/-- `V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` holds iff every value of `σ` indexes a cover member containing `V`;
i.e. `σ` factors through `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`.  This is the index-splitting criterion separating
the surviving summands of the evaluated free Čech complex from the vanishing ones. -/
lemma le_coverInterOpen_iff (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)
    {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
    V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ↔ ∀ k, V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) := by
  simp only [coverInterOpen, le_iInf_iff]

/-- **Reindexing the surviving multi-indices.** The multi-indices `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` with
`V ≤ U_σ` are in bijection with the maps `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)` into the subtype `I₁(V) = {i : V ≤ U_i}`
of cover members containing `V`.  This bijection feeds `Limits.Sigma.whiskerEquiv` to collapse the
surviving coproduct onto the constant-coefficient combinatorial index `Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`. -/
def survivingEquiv (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
    (Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) ≃
      {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} where
  toFun τ := ⟨fun k => (τ k).1, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V _).2 fun k => (τ k).2⟩
  invFun σ := fun k => ⟨σ.1 k, (le_coverInterOpen_iff 𝒰 V σ.1).1 σ.2 k⟩
  left_inv τ := by funext k
  right_inv σ := by ext k; rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-- **Drop-zeros isomorphism.** Evaluating the degree-`p` free Čech term at `V` and splitting off the
vanishing summands: the full coproduct over all `σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀` is isomorphic to the
coproduct over the *surviving* multi-indices `{σ : V ≤ U_σ}`, because every non-surviving summand
`(eval V)(freeYoneda U_σ)` (with `V ≰ U_σ`) is a zero object
(`freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le`).  This is the first of the two halves identifying the evaluated
term with the combinatorial model. -/
noncomputable def cechFreeEvalDropZeros (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
    (∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) ≅
      (∐ fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) where
  hom := Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ =>
    if h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ then
      Limits.Sigma.ι (fun s : {σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ} =>
        (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
          (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 s.1))) ⟨σ, h⟩
    else 0
  inv := Limits.Sigma.desc fun s =>
    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
      (PresheafOfModules.evaluation X.ringCatSheaf.obj (Opposite.op V)).obj
        (freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) s.1
  hom_inv_id := by
    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro σ
    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id]
    by_cases h : V ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ
    · rw [dif_pos h, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]
    · rw [dif_neg h, Limits.zero_comp]
      exact ((freeYonedaEval_isZero_of_not_le h).eq_zero_of_src _).symm
  inv_hom_id := by
    apply Limits.Sigma.hom_ext; intro s
    rw [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc_assoc, Category.comp_id, Limits.Sigma.ι_desc, dif_pos s.2]

end AlgebraicGeometry

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the engine chain complex `C•`

The degreewise model `∐_{τ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)` assembles into an honest chain complex of
`O_X(V)`-modules, the **engine complex** `cechEngineComplex`.  Its differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p` is the
alternating sum of the index-dropping coproduct reindexings `σ ↦ σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i` — this is the
chain (insertion) form of the Stacks `lemma-homology-complex` differential, the chain dual of the
constant-coefficient `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`.  `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` (next) identifies
the evaluated free Čech complex with `cechEngineComplex`; this section provides its target. -/

/-- The constant coefficient module `O_X(V)` viewed as a module over itself.  This is the per-summand
target of `freeYonedaEval_iso_of_le`, hence the coefficient of the engine complex. -/
abbrev coverSectionModule (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) :
    ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
  ModuleCat.of _ (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V))

/-- The degree-`p` object of the engine complex: `∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)} O_X(V)`. -/
abbrev cechEngineX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
    ModuleCat (X.ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op V)) :=
  ∐ fun _ : Fin (p + 1) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V

/-- The engine differential `C_{p+1} ⟶ C_p`: the alternating sum over `i : Fin (p+2)` of the
coproduct reindexing maps `ι_σ ↦ (-1)^i • ι_{σ ∘ Fin.succAbove i}` that drop the `i`-th index of the
multi-index `σ : Fin (p+2) → I₁(V)`.  Chain (insertion) dual of `FreeCechEngine.combDifferential`. -/
noncomputable def cechEngineD (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X) (p : ℕ) :
    cechEngineX 𝒰 V (p + 1) ⟶ cechEngineX 𝒰 V p :=
  Limits.Sigma.desc fun σ => ∑ i : Fin (p + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) •

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — contracting homotopy of the engine complex

Fixing an index `i_fix ∈ I₁(V)` (which exists exactly when `V` meets some cover member), the
prepend-`i_fix` map `ι_σ ↦ ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}` contracts the engine complex: `d ∘ s + s ∘ d = id`
in every positive degree.  This is the chain (coproduct) port of `FreeCechEngine.combHomotopy` /
`combHomotopy_spec`, and it yields the positive-degree exactness of `cechEngineComplex` feeding the
nonempty case of the free Čech quasi-isomorphism. -/

/-- The prepend-`i_fix` contracting map `C_p ⟶ C_{p+1}` of the engine complex: on the coproduct
injection `ι_σ` (`σ : Fin (p+1) → I₁(V)`) it returns `ι_{Fin.cons i_fix σ}`. -/

    (i_fix : {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i}) (p : ℕ) :

    Limits.Sigma.ι (fun _ : Fin (p + 2) → {i : 𝒰.I₀ // V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i} => coverSectionModule V)
      (Fin.cons i_fix σ)

    Limits.Sigma.ι _ σ ≫ cechEnginePrepend 𝒰 V i_fix p
